# County of Armagnac
Under Roman rule, the lands of Armagnac were under the lands of Aquitania. Under the Merovingain dynasty, the lands stayed under
events.fullText:
# County of Armagnac
Under Roman rule, the lands of Armagnac were under the lands of Aquitania. Under the Merovingain dynasty, the lands stayed under the duchy of Aquitania until a hereditary county split into the lands known as [Fezensac](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/County_of_Fézensac).

In 960, Armagnac, under Bernard le Louche, became a seperate county. In the 1040 campaign to reunite Gascony, Bernard the II reunited multiple counties, including Armagnac, under the county of Gascony.

Increased pressures from French nobility and the Crown for tithes and county allegiance, as tensions with England enflame, have made the Gascon region disgruntled and wary of French orders. Loyalty stirs at an all time low.

events.author: Bear
events.snippet:
# Centule IV

Followed by the untimely death of Joan d'Armagnac on Februrary 17th, 1337, a young Centule IV of Astarac has claimed the Armagnac seat.
events.fullText:
# Centule IV

Followed by the untimely death of Joan d'Armagnac on Februrary 17th, 1337, a young Centule IV of Astarac has claimed the Armagnac seat. Through his mother, Mathe d'Armagnac, daughter of Geraud II, viscount of Fezensac, the power vacuum became sealed with control of the county.

Centule IV also maintains claims, through maternal heritage, to the counties Astarac and Foix. His distant relative, Bernat the 5th, sent message to his mother that, when he comes of age, Centule IV should take the seat in Astarac, but plots and schemes by the French nobles to turn the southern counties over to Northern French bloodlines has resulted in the seat being taken by the de Comminges family and its bastard heir.

Centule the 4th remains warry of the French nobility, unlike his members of the court who despise the ruling class. He has taken over in a time of strife, and at such a young age. Time will tell how agreeable the southern provinces may remain

events.author: Bear
events.snippet:
## Armagnac Disloyalty

Count Centule IV is wary of your campaign, King Phillip. I grow tired of my brothers and fathers blood being split for French
events.fullText:
## Armagnac Disloyalty

Count Centule IV is wary of your campaign, King Phillip. I grow tired of my brothers and fathers blood being split for French power and dominance. I grow weary of false promises of the stoppage of noble sackings of my holdings for "tithes"

If the King wishes the county of Armagnac to march for Gascon's freedom, then he must also receive adequate compensation for his people's blood, toil, and strife. Those in the North will no nothing of the pain and blood shed in the South of France

||If the campaign is successful, the County of Armagnac will be returned his rightful claim of the county of Astarac under his hereditary rights, as well the spoils and lands of anything captured.||

If these terms are not agreeable, you might find some other compromise, lest the English King offers a better deal

Your Vassal,

Centule IV

**(General hostilities and distrustworthiness due to established Dice Rolls)**

events.author: Caleb
events.snippet: Most Holy Father <@150411715757080576>, with the war between France and England drawing nearer by the day, We most humbly petition Your Holiness to is
events.fullText: Most Holy Father <@150411715757080576>, with the war between France and England drawing nearer by the day, We most humbly petition Your Holiness to issue a Papal Ban prohibiting either crown from drawing the princes, cities, and peoples of the Imperial Lowlands into their dynastic quarrel by force, levy, or alliance, upon pain of interdict — that the looms, wharfs, and faithful flocks of these lands be spared a ruin that would wound the Church Herself. His Grace the Prince-Bishop of Cologne <@112960565344804864> has consented to lend his seal in support of this cause, and We, as ever an obedient son of Rome, commend this petition to Your Holiness's wisdom and beg Your Apostolic Benediction.

events.author: HK je Srbija

https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1506063474017701929



The Orsini delegate is welcomed at Wawel Castle, the seat o
events.fullText:
https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1506063474017701929



The Orsini delegate is welcomed at Wawel Castle, the seat of power for the Polish monarchy. An unusual situation has emerged where the Bishop of Krakow Jan Grot has excommunicated King Casimir III for preferring negotiations with the Teutonic Order for Dobrzyń Land and Kuyavia rather then war. The king has subsequently accused the the Bishop of revealing state secrets, inciting rebellion, and abusing ecclesiastical benefices and has previously written to the Pope to request that pressure be put on the bishop to relent with his obstinancy. 

This is only the first matter to discuss. King Casimir also hopes to bring up the matter of the negotiations with the Teutonic Order to gain papal support for the Teutons to end their occupation. Furthermore there is the Lithuanian question, where the king hopes that through Polish diplomacy and tact Christianisation can be achieved rather then through the sword as promoted by the Germans. 

Much to discuss. But first, a feast in Wawel Castle! Pheasants, boars, bison and wine will be prepared for the noble man of Orsini!

events.fullText:
Ivan I starts the process of approaching the ryad and the nobles of Muscovy-Vladimir, to once and for all bring down Tver and the son of the murderer of his father and end this bloody feud. Tver will submit unto the Grand Principality, or it will perish.

Upon the grand city Ivan I, incorruptible and careful, makes preparations, plans of attack. The Veche is already notified of it, as troops are gathered as quietly as they can, though still perceptible. The Ryad debates in the halls of the Moscow keep, how to best approach this.

events.snippet:
The Udmurts and the Chiyaliks

The inclusion of the Udmurts and Chiyaliks in Perm has long been a contentious topic and a gathering has been called in
events.fullText:
The Udmurts and the Chiyaliks

The inclusion of the Udmurts and Chiyaliks in Perm has long been a contentious topic and a gathering has been called in Ćerdin to clear up the matter. After many days of negotiation an agreement has finally been reached. The Udmurts have decided to establish their own region as clearly seperate from Perm, yet they see the advantage of banding together under Prince Yövgin' and has elected them as his prince as well, with a continued vow of friendship with the Komi they hope their lands will prosper - even if somewhat more seperate than before.

They heave, ho and vomit. Their mouths are filled with rats and locusts. Their teeth fall out. Their eyes wriggle with maggots,
events.fullText:
## SICKNESS INCARNATE
They heave, ho and vomit. Their mouths are filled with rats and locusts. Their teeth fall out. Their eyes wriggle with maggots, putrid infested beastly monstrous insectoid things. Reeking of death and shit, they sneeze, sneeze, first snot, then water, then blood, and then lastly flesh, til they do not sneeze no more.

events.author: brainamoeba
events.snippet: The Hanseatic League currently exerts a great amount of influence over the Baltic sea. This influence, alongside historic monetary support to the orde
events.fullText:
The Hanseatic League currently exerts a great amount of influence over the Baltic sea. This influence, alongside historic monetary support to the order, creates the basis of good relations between the various Wendish cities and the Prussian and Livonian trading cities of Danzig, Konigsberg and Riga.

Teutonic commercial interests in England and the Low Countries also require significant cooperation with Hanseatic ports and fleets. Likewise, Hanseatic interests in Novgorod's lumber require ports in the eastern Baltic sea. Let us then seek cooperation and prosperity for all the cities in the Baltic!

events.fullText:
Rise of the Ushkuyniks

The Udmurt, Komi and Mari peoples inhabiting Vyatka Land have long been good friends with the peoples of Perm, but recent history goes against the grain. Bands of Novgorodian pirates known as *Ushkuyniks* have established themselves on the Upper Volga - subjugating native peoples and raiding without mercy... should this state of affairs continue the Komi may have to take action to support their brethren peoples in Vyatka Land.

**Defensive Measures in Eastern Europe: The Treaty of Poznan**
With the threat of *certain* powers in eastern Europe becoming increasingly apparent, Poland and Denmark have signed a mutual defensive agreement to protect against foreign aggression. While this "Treaty of Poznan" does not include any provisions for offensive wars, perhaps with time the relationship can grow closer.

Grand Prince Semyon keeps the latest policy of his father in keeping friendly relations with the Novgorodians and the Veche.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**In Defense of the People**

Long have the Komi people hoped for a peaceful resolution to the situation in Vyatka land but it seems the hopes of the
events.fullText:
**In Defense of the People**

Long have the Komi people hoped for a peaceful resolution to the situation in Vyatka land but it seems the hopes of the common folk have been for not; brigands for Novgorods have established themselves fully in Vyatka, erecting stockades in Vyatka town. Meanwhile their raids only grow more ambitous, more greedy, and after a raid deep into Perm lands along the Kama a council has been called in Ćerdin to resolve the issue - the consensus is clear:

*Vyatka must be pacified!*

The Komi chieftains are in agreement, and with the approval of the Udmurts, Besermyans and Chikyalik leaders the course of war has been decided upon. When the troops are ready prince Yövgin' will lead the greatest warriors west of the Urals down to the Vyatka to put an end to the greed of these scum of Novgorod.

(<@160455024994942976> they aren't official Novgorodians or whatever but I imagine the state might take an interest in the affairs of these pirates either way. They're also allied with Beloozero so that might have consequences.)

events.fullText:
# BOHEMIA ARMS RISE TO RESTORE THE RULE OF RUDOLF II VON WITTELSBACH # 

<@261290667106369536> 
<@763652399784919080> 
<@190640828954181632> 
<@564196329132261376> 

With the blessing of the Kaiser, Rudolf II amounts a large force to retake his lands and destroy his Brother Ruprecht "the Red" and his scheemer with the Habsburgs. 

On the other side Ruprecht has found allies with the Savoians, through an old treaty signed by his older brother. 

But perhaps the largest point of support for Ruprecht is the support from Austria. There the Habsburgs stand by his side, for aiding him in his Election bid for the Holy Roman Empire. 

In the Heart of the Holy Roman Empire troops gather, for a large war is now coming up. Will the forces of Kaiser Ludwig IV win in this intervention or shall the Habsburg-Red alliance succeed in defending the new Ruler?

## Ascension of Count Otto I von Habsburg 
Following a period of interregnum following the death of the heirless Count Guillaume d'Avesnes, the good burgher and Regent Alfried Meijer began considering the major houses of the Holy Roman Empire to invite a scion to rule in Den Haag to ensure the stability and prosperity of the realm. As the succession of the Empire draws near, ally of the late Count and Dioces of the Western Empire the Bishop-Elector of Cologne <@112960565344804864> supports Duke Albrecht II von Habsburg <@190640828954181632> for the imperial throne and with the support of other electors appears the favorite. With this in mind, the regent offers the seat to the Duke's brother, and with the Emperor-Apparent's blessing and financial support secured Otto now rules over the County.

**Under the agreement:**

1.Avignon shall no longer
events.fullText:
**## Orsini have made a deal with the King Philip VI of France <:rose:865579087883272192> **
@here
**Under the agreement:**

1.Avignon shall no longer remain under Church control and shall instead be granted to King Philip VI.

2. The excommunication of King Philip VI shall be revoked.

3. King Edward III of England shall be excommunicated for declaring a war of aggression against the French Crown.

4. Luigi Orsini shall reside in Paris in good faith and mutual agreement.

With this agreement, the Schism will end, and peace shall be secured. There will be no bloodshed.

events.author: Tayta
events.snippet:
A sealed letter arrives at the court of **Muscovy**, carried by riders from **Kyiv **beneath the banner of **Prince Fedir Riurykovych.**

|| *“To the
events.fullText:
A sealed letter arrives at the court of **Muscovy**, carried by riders from **Kyiv **beneath the banner of **Prince Fedir Riurykovych.**

|| *“To the Princes and Boyars of Muscovy, *

*For too long the lands of Rus’ have lived beneath the shadow of the Tatar yoke. Our princes are made to bow before foreign khans, our people burdened with tribute, and our birthright denied by those who neither share our faith nor our blood.*

*Across the lands of Rus’, the drums of change grow louder with every passing season. The people yearn for freedom, and many princes now look toward the future with fire in their hearts rather than fear. Though differences may stand between our realms, we remain Slavic brothers, heirs to the same ancient legacy, bound together by history, faith, and the memory of our forefathers.
*
*I ask that Muscovy stand beside Kyiv in common cause, so that together we may cast down the rule of the Horde and restore dignity to the princes of Rus’.*

*United, we may achieve what none of us could alone. " * ||

**— Prince Fedir Riurykovych, Prince of Kyiv**
<@307587752860581888>

events.author: Tayta
events.snippet:
Word of the riches of the west continued to reach the halls of **Kyiv **through wandering merchants and foreign travelers. 

Tales were told of **silk
events.fullText:
Word of the riches of the west continued to reach the halls of **Kyiv **through wandering merchants and foreign travelers. 

Tales were told of **silks **from distant lands, finely woven garments(**fine cloth**), exotic **fruits **unseen in the markets of Rus’, and rare spices(**saffron**) worth their weight in silver. 

Such luxuries soon caught the attention of **Prince Fedir**, who understood that wealth alone was not their only value. To stand among the courts of Europe, **Kyiv **would need not only strength, but prestige.

Of all possible markets, **Constantinople **appeared the most natural choice. The shared **Orthodox **faith offered a bond stronger than mere coin, and **Fedir **believed that friendship with the **Byzantine **merchants could bring both prosperity and legitimacy to his growing ambitions.

The lands of **Kyiv **had long been rich in **grain**, **timber**, **coal**, **wax**, and **agricultural goods**, resources greatly valued across the **Black Sea** trade routes. Seeing an opportunity, the prince ordered letters to be sent southward toward the **burgher guilds** and merchant houses of the **Byzantine world**, offering favorable trade agreements in exchange for luxury goods and closer relations between their peoples.

Now the court awaited their answer, hopeful that commerce might succeed where armies could not.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## The Battle of Mosbach

As the forces of King Karel and the forces of the late Kaiser Ludwig IV of Upper Bavaria moved to make battle against Ruprec
events.fullText:
## The Battle of Mosbach

As the forces of King Karel and the forces of the late Kaiser Ludwig IV of Upper Bavaria moved to make battle against Ruprecht the Red, the combined armies of the sides, embroiling the Empire in a power struggle as the electors stood divided—two Anti-Kings fielded against one another at once. Karel himself was at the field of battle, and led his army to victory, and his claim to the Empire enforced by Rudolf II’s imposition as Count-Palatine of the Rhine, at the cost of being disfigured in the battle.

Through battle, Karel has become **King of the Germans**, but must deal with the remaining resistance of the Italians, of Albrecht II, and of the Orsini aligned clergy in Germany. 

<@215639063502258176> <@306458253674545153> <@763652399784919080> <@137101075411894272> <@190640828954181632> <@792904515795550218> <@385875387608989714> <@112960565344804864> <@222439858927435776> <@271318019052011532>

events.author: GMB
events.snippet:
# THE BATTLE OF MOSBACH - THE DISFIGUREMENT OF KING KAREL AND THE CROWNING OF KAREL AS KING OF THE GERMANS # 

When the forces of the Red-Habsburgs de
events.fullText:
# THE BATTLE OF MOSBACH - THE DISFIGUREMENT OF KING KAREL AND THE CROWNING OF KAREL AS KING OF THE GERMANS # 

When the forces of the Red-Habsburgs defeated Moravian Troops at the battle of Amberg, Heidelberg began to be placed under Siege. Quickly the forces were sent to stop the siege. With Mainz troops and Savoian men supporting the battle, the two armies met in Mosbach. 

Although the forces of the Red-Habsburgs were outmanouvered by the Bohemian-Bavarian men, the battle still was close. So close that the King of Bohemia was struck down. Not dead but disfigured in the midst of battle. 

Yet this was not enough to fight back the genius of King Karel. The local forces would quickly be defeated and the Bohemian-Bavarian Alliance would win the battle. 

Yet surprisingly the "Red" Wittelsbach would redeem himself. Having become a battlefield Medic, King Karel was saved from certain death. And with it the war and battle was over. Rudolf II was once again the Ruler of the Palatinate and King Karel, when news arrived that Kaiser Ludwig IV had died, was quickly crowned King of the Germans. 

**Crowned through battle the new soon to be Kaiser was Crowned: Long live Kaiser Karel IV of Luxemburg**

<@261290667106369536> <@190640828954181632> <@222439858927435776> <@306458253674545153> <@763652399784919080>

## Karel’s Coronation 

In the backdrop of an approaching pestilence yet to reach monumental Aachen, and in the wake of the Battle of Mosbach, Karel of Bohemia has attended and been affirmed in his appropriate royal regalia as **King of the Germans**, despite ongoing opposition.

## The Bohemian Regency

Karel IV’s death, weakened from his disfigurement from Mosbach,  was taken by the satanic witchery of the unholy enemies agai
events.fullText:
## The Bohemian Regency

Karel IV’s death, weakened from his disfigurement from Mosbach,  was taken by the satanic witchery of the unholy enemies against him. With his passing, and no adult child left to inherit by way of election, a party of the local Bohemian aristocracy led by **Zbygněv z Landštejn** has organized to govern the country while **kingless**. 

Now various parties gather across the realm to discuss and send missives to other dynasties of who may be elected the next king, as the electoral privileges should forbid a long-term regency…

The clearest Luxembourg candidate is Jan Jindřich, **King-Consort of Poland**, but will they vote for him? Will other contenders arise?

# What remains

Corpses lie the streets, the rivers and the fields. We have paid a great penance upon our land, those who prayed, cried to the almighty lord, have survived this wrath from the heavens. We must show our devotion for which we have not proved ourselves enough.

As the barren lands must regrow, we will now lift those who have fallen, the church has declared a mission of regrowth for the ill fortuned who will show true faith.

Another opportunity strikes, as the city lays in chaos, order may yet be restored in the Prince-Bishops name for which we can regrow this city of the Rhine

**Breton Marches Aflame**

After years of internal skirmishes and town sackings between the Blois and Montfort Factions the king of France would respond to the Breton civil war with drawing in their banners to march in the name of John de Montfort. 

Nantes would halfheartedly defend against an army of ten thousand under the command Phillipe of Burgundy however after only a handful of weeks the city traders and municipal governors would open the gates with agreements of avoiding exeuctions from the crown of france. Charlez Blois forced to flee into the Armorican Massif or face the full weight of royal forces.

<@460814348487753730> <@1106651545296195655> 
> ***“For God's sake leav
events.fullText:
**Duke John III ‘the Good’ is dead, the Breton War of succession has begun**  
<@460814348487753730> <@1106651545296195655> 
> ***“For God's sake leave me alone and do not trouble my spirit with such things” ***
- Duke John III last words when asked who should succeed him

 the plague left by the wars in Aquitaine would spread not only to the poor but the so called Godly, though for Duke John he had become nothing like a Godly man for some time. In his palace at Rennes he would die in maddened fits screaming at his vassals, brought about it is said by his wife's miscarriage of a son, leaving only a daughter born, making his now succession even more complex.

Leaving behind his wife Jiana of Savoy and self claimed lady of Savoy <@564196329132261376> , daughter Janned, his half brother John de Montfort and Blois married to his niece Jeanne de Penthièvre and the realm in shock. 

in such chaos the Paris parliament would announce their support (with Phillips pre-planned blessing) for surprising Montfort and Johns Niece, the two leaving for Paris to accept their recognition, though Blois enraged by the kings war with the papacy would have already left. 

***Now in 1342 Anno Domini the Lord Blois and his wife have in the name of the three year old Daughter of the former duke Janned have seized the city of Nantes, Rennes and Saint Marlo without resistance, the lords and cities governments unable to respond in such chaos. Already moving to contact king Edward the III and the English Parliament for support against the wicked french king while Montfort will require the royal french army to seize back their lands.***

Meanwhile with the Ducal power being seized Charlez Blois and his wife swiftly sends messages with the ducal seal across France calling upon vassals to fight back against royal interference against customary law <@259426601865838592><@696548460094029914> .While the holy knight calls upon the pope who was spited by the french king for support of coin or anything else betwen <@150411715757080576> .

May god have mercy upon Brittany for the french king shall surely have none.

One more crown left... or maybe not?

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**The Combat of Thirty **
<@460814348487753730><@1106651545296195655><@696548460094029914> 
<@1417260821674852363>

The Turn Around of French forces sees the Breton war of Succession collapse into a frantic mess of siege, counter siege, roving warbands and mercenary forces from Burgundy and Scotland. To bring order to this chaos in true spirit of the Carolingian cycle Charlez an Blois issues a challenge to the King of France and his Dukes on Campaign, a battle of Thirty Knights, fifteen on either side ever a chivalric romantic. Yet shockingly John De Montfort would accept either out to honour his half brothers memory and wanting to stop the heavy fighting or his own amicability.

At Guillac knights of both sides including the Black Prince, Charles Blois, John de Montfort, Phillipe Bourgogne among many others would duel. 

A Scene that would go on to inspire artwork and song these knights with lance, sword and mace would hack and slash upon each other. The Montfort Royal knights would eventually in the chaotic clash be rallied by the Young Loyalist Bertrand Guesclin to break their rivals formation and force Blois to yield the battle, Bertrand killing a grand total of three knights of Blois including the English commander Hugh Calveley, his black eagle crest bloodied and leading to them declaring this young brutal man the **Black Eagle**

Yet despite the french factions victory it would not be without grave cost. Edward the black prince fighting through the melee as the french rallied towards John de Montfort would isolate and duel him while Charles held back the French forces. the dramatic battle would see the black prince slay the leader of the French faction of the civil war, in his last moments declaring

> *"i was never so loved as i am now"*

France claim victory yet with their leader gone the host would be forced to withdraw, Charles calling **Edward the gift of Ankou**.

events.snippet: The good Alderman and his entourage are welcomed with a feast in Den Haag at the Binnenhof, with negotiations scheduled for the following day. ||The B
events.fullText:
The good Alderman and his entourage are welcomed with a feast in Den Haag at the Binnenhof, with negotiations scheduled for the following day. ||The Burger is seated to the right of the Count so they may make each others acquaintance and affirm his status as an honored guest. The dinner is primarily domestic - dominated by venison, beef, poultry, and fish traditionally cooked and seasoned - however vintage Rhenish and Bordeaux wine and various foreign spirits like English Whiskey and Norwegian Brennevin serve to nod to the existing trade relations the merchants of Holland have built over the decades across Western Europe. Count Guillaume is man in his fifth decade of life, having seen several battlefields in his time and ten fold as many negotiations he as amicable as he is confident from his seat at the head of the high table as its host. The Alderman and his men are quartered within the Binnenhof and after their fast is broken negotiations begin in earnest.||

||The Count takes in the Hanseatic offer, confers with his advisors for a short time, and replies with his position. The strategic position of Dordrecht at the mouth of the Rhein and the booming textile industry across the county creates a demand for the moving of goods that our merchants simply cannot fulfill at present. The more English wool that can reach our ports the more cloth we can put on the market, the more wealth we have to invest back into the realm. We are intent on remaining neutral in the coming conflict between London and Paris and will be seeking direct Papal and Imperial decrees to protect the Imperial Lowlands from the ravages such a conflict might bring, we have personally seen the destruction wars a fraction of the size can bring and want no part in it for my subjects or towns. The Duchy of Flanders as a subject of the King of France in Paris would not be subject to any such protections or restrictions, and it does not take a strategist to see Brugge is directly within the path of this war. The Hansa may have its pick of the choice lots within our towns to build its trade posts, should we see a noticeable uptick in beneficial trade as a result we'd certainly consider permitting the establishment of Kontors within our realm. Hansa merchants will find safe harbor at our ports and a bottomless market for raw fibers to spin in the looms, and we hope this is the beginning of a long and lucrative relationship between our realm and the League.||

events.snippet:
## The Mad Charge of the 70, Trier at the Battle of Arques

The French seizure of Gascony was not to be decided in Aquitaine after the 400 day siege o
events.fullText:
## The Mad Charge of the 70, Trier at the Battle of Arques

The French seizure of Gascony was not to be decided in Aquitaine after the 400 day siege of Bastogne. But instead it was to be decided in the forests of Arques, a quaint coastal area on the Channel Coast. It would be here were the two great Armies of the Righteous French King and the traitorous, mad and murderous English King would met. 
And amongst that french 50,000 were 70 Noble Men and their Squires from the lands of the Bishop of Trier. Honoring a promise the late Baldwin of Luxembourg made to the French King in payment for the Sanctuary he provided Baldwin.

With the very sudden death of Baldwin of Luxembourg, **Sir Bernhard Siegmund von Brunshorn**, was tasked to lead the detachment of Trier to the French Kings aid. And it was the Nobles of Trier that he would leave, and exclusively the Nobles of Trier that would fight

And it was these 70 men that opened the battle of Arques. Tasked with seeking glory, and honoring a dead mans last wish they did just that. The Noble men from Trier were the first of the French to charge, and charge so early and so fast they did that some foolish scholars consider the Charge of the 70 to be a separate battle. But it was not a Separate Battle, **no it was the route to French Victory**. With the 70 Charging headlong and drawing the first volley of English Longbows it gave space for the French Center to not be cut down by the English arrows to break though both the Center and Left flank of the English Army. Winning the Battle for King Phillipe de Valois 

While the Mad 70 of Trier lay dead to a man, never reaching the French line and their corpses trampled under the oncoming French charge. It is without a doubt in Trier that these Mad 70 were the men that won the Battle of Arques for France, for Trier, for Baldwin and for all that is Good and Honest in this World.
<@460814348487753730> <@1106651545296195655>

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# Guy II de Lusignan, the ambitious King of Cyprus and King of Jerusalem

Guy, the heir presumptive of the Kingdoms of Cyprus and Jerusalem since his
events.fullText:
# Guy II de Lusignan, the ambitious King of Cyprus and King of Jerusalem

Guy, the heir presumptive of the Kingdoms of Cyprus and Jerusalem since his birth, was in Limassol attending a noble's feast when news arrived of his father's death. Hugh IV was another victim of the Great Plague, which swept across Cyprus and killed tens of thousands. Hugh proved an ill equipped King to deal with the crisis, and due to his ineffective governance the nobles of Cyprus were near revolt by the end of his reign, but his death calmed the waters more than anything else he could do. On June 30th 1348, after having attended to his father's funeral, Guy de Lusignan received the crown of Cyprus in Nicosie. Weeks later, he also received the crown of Jerusalem in Famagusta, continuing the tradition started by his father.

Right away Guy II proved his stable leadership by rapidly repairing relations with the Frankish nobles and knights in the Kingdom, reforming the laws of the Kingdom to integrate the elites more closely into the royal court and increasing the nobles power over the serfs and courts. In addition to the great effort to tie the nobility loyally to the King, Guy II finally developed a renewed and ambitious diplomatic policy that Hugh IV seemed incapable of formulating ever since the failure of 1336. As much as could be allowed by the Kingdom's meager resources, Cypriot raids increased on the southern Anatolian coast, against both the Turks and the Armenian Kingdom of Cilicia. Yet Guy knew he would need aid from the west if he wanted to establish any kind of permanent presence in the region, and as such the diplomatic efforts of the fierce negotiator King are sure to step up in the near future.

Beyond foreign and domestic policy changes, Guy II would also deal with an issue that also plagued his father, that of marriage.

Like his father, Guy's wife died young. The Black Death had taken her, and by the time she died she had only given Guy one son and one daughter. Guy II elected not to remarry, both out of respect for his departed wife, and because he was confident that his young son would grow into a fine King. Unfortunately, God had already decided young Prince Hugh's fate. In July 1353, the heir presumptive became ill, and it took a rapid turn for the worse. Just a few weeks later and Prince Hugh was dead, taken by God at only eighteen years old.

Struck by grief, it took many months for King Guy to make a decision on whether he would remarry and try again for an heir. Despite his many brothers pleading for him to remain unmarried (and thus ensuring that one of them would inherit the throne), Guy had found a woman whom he had fallen in love with, a local Cypriot beauty named Anna. His mind was made up, and he sent a letter to Pope John XXIII requesting a Papal dispensation to remarry. Having received such permission, Guy had Anna crowned as his Queen. The nobility was irked by this marriage, but Guy's previous efforts at winning them over ensured that there was no major disturbance at this. Furthermore, although Anna was a native Cypriot, she was a convert to Catholicism, and so it wasn't seen as too great an insult to the Franks.

The marriage quickly proved a success, with Guy II seeming genuinely in love with the Queen. So much so in fact that the Bishop of Limassol, Peter Dubois would write that:

*"So for the love which King Guy had promised that, wherever he was, he would take Queen Anna's shift (night-dress) to lay it at night in his arms when he slept, and he made his chamberlain always bring with him the queen's shift, and had him put it in his bed."*

In 1354, the new royal couple would be glad to announce to the Kingdom the birth of a new heir, Peter, on the day of Jesus' birth, the 25th of December. A good omen, all in the Kingdom would think.

With the economy of Cyprus strong despite the Black Death, and the active recruitment of professional soldiers and sailors, it would appear that the Kingdom is ready to make a move, if it can find the allies it needs. If one thing is to be assured, it is that King Guy II de Lusignan will act with the fierceness of a lion, and the faith of a true crusader King.

For God wills it.

-# King Guy II of Cyprus and Jerusalem

-# The Noble Reforms of Guy II

<@150411715757080576> <@170593650584059904> <@302128861209165824> <@874750350538727434>

**(End)**

events.fullText:
**To the Kingdom of Aragon**


An envoy from Tunis has come to Barcelona on behalf of the young **Crown Prince Abu Bakr Hafsid**, seeking to deliver a message to King Pere IV of Aragon.  It is in relation to the recent conflict in Sicily, and the continued threat posed by the Neapolitans and their Genoese allies–making their union of mutual concern.

Of note, no noble of House Barcelona from Sicily has been harmed, with all being granted permission to stay and maintain their holdings in Sicily, under the Sultanate's rule, or return to Aragon, as befits their desires.

Will the envoy be received?

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# a Reciprocal Visit.

In January 1356, a letter would find its way to Constantinople. The messenger who delivered it would do so with all the expecte
events.fullText:
# a Reciprocal Visit.

In January 1356, a letter would find its way to Constantinople. The messenger who delivered it would do so with all the expected manner of respect and deference. It was for the Emperor, he would claim. The seal of the parchment was adorned with a lion, the sigil of the Lusignans. Opening it would read:

*"To his Imperial Majesty, the faithful John, Emperor of Constantinople and ruler of the Aegean.

The grace of God, His peace and His good be with you! 

Years ago I traveled as Prince of Galilee to the holy city of Constantine to meet with his Roman Majesty, Andronikos III. With the exalted glory of the merciful God we reached an understanding between our realms, that of the Romans and the Franks.

Now I offer you the invitation to visit upon mine own realm, where we may meet and by divine counsel the friendship between I the King and your Majesty shall be reaffirmed, and God shall be praised by all.

Our just restoration of brotherly love shall reach the borders of Christianity and our common enemies will be annihilated, and our common friends saved.

--- Guy II de Lusignan, King of Cyprus and King of Jerusalem."*

<@302128861209165824>

events.author: JPThunda
events.snippet:
**The Conquest of Sicily–The Aftermath**


As a matter of practicality, little changed in Sicily.  Although the Kingdom was now under Muslim administr
events.fullText:
**The Conquest of Sicily–The Aftermath**


As a matter of practicality, little changed in Sicily.  Although the Kingdom was now under Muslim administration, the power structures and the nobility were left untouched.  Nobles of House Barcelona that wished to depart to Aragon were given free leave to do so, and those who wished to retain their station within the local power structures were freed to do so.  With this new, greater Dhimmi community, new laws and protections were discussed, including the establishment of **Guaranteed Local Land Rights, Legal Autonomy, & Mazalim Courts**--secular courts to administer to the Dimmi outside the confines of Shar’ia Law.  There were even talks of permitting the Dhimmi into cabinet positions at court, opening the way for future **Dimmi Bureaucrats** within these **Abrahamic Communities**.  These new policies would take some time to implement however, due to ongoing attempts by the Burghers to reform the **Mejlis** into a more representative parliament–although this would certainly give the Dhimmi more representation as well, through their common cause with the Burghers.  This would also be frustrated by the death of **Sultan Uthmar Abu Hafs Hafsid II** on the 21st of August, 1352.  It was said that the shock of their total victory was so complete that he had suffered a heart attack, leaving **Crown Prince Abu Bakr Hafsid** to inherit the kingdom.

Outwardly, the Sultanate is in a precarious position.  The enraged Kingdom of Naples is sure to plot their next attempt on the island, and Tunis has made itself clear and present force in the Mediterranean, perhaps ending its long seclusion in Africa before it was entirely ready.  Similarly the Genoese are no doubt incensed, having been denied their prize in Sardinia.  News has already spread that the most recent Pope of Rome intends some form of retribution, though the time and form of this is yet unclear.  The future of the Sultanate will be a tumultuous one, indeed.

## Prince-Bishop Waldemar the Cruel

Waldemar was elected by the Cathedral Chapter of Trier. Waldemar was the Elder and most established candidate of
events.fullText:
## Prince-Bishop Waldemar the Cruel

Waldemar was elected by the Cathedral Chapter of Trier. Waldemar was the Elder and most established candidate of the three candidates, all of them lowborn. Waldemar never sought the support of the Orsini papacy. But with the rapid fall from grace, a fall to hell even of Orsini power with the Orsini loss to the French. Waldemar didn't need to seek support from the Orsini.

Nor has Waldemar sought the support of the [incorruptible Ioannes XIII](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545415213449216/1508220938519449802) for one simple reason. Waldemar is **cruel **man, going so far as to be **obsessive** in that cruelty. His cruel passions are all Waldemar that knows, thinks and dreams. The first rumors of Waldmars sadism first started to spread in the Moselle valley, when Waldemar did not make any attempt to "Sponsor Mass Forgiveness" during the Emperors Plague. So unmoved by the plight of the common folk while safely residing in the **Trier Cathedral tower**. They say Waldemar enjoyed to look out his window and watch the plague victims dying in agony. And that would only be the start of the local rumors of Waldemars **sadism**. 

 In truth Waldemar should consider himself lucky that Ioannes has not yet visited Trier during his pilgrimage of the German Holy sties, Aachen, [Cologne](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505974152194953289/1508227180558028911). For if Ioannes decided to visit Trier, Waldemar not being a secretive man would be hard pressed **to hide his sadistic passions from the Pope**. Nor would Waldemar a terrible diplomat at the best of times be able to argue his position with the Pontiff or his agents.

On the Unresolved Affairs of Metz

For a long time, the Moselle region has suffered from an unstable situation, threatening trade and prosperity throu
events.fullText:
On the Unresolved Affairs of Metz

For a long time, the Moselle region has suffered from an unstable situation, threatening trade and prosperity throughout the lands.

These tensions can be traced back to the War of the Four Lords, which left behind divisions and an open wound within the region. In order to restore lasting peace and stability, Lorraine now sees it as necessary to address the unresolved affairs of Metz.

The lands of the Pays Messin surrounding Metz have for centuries maintained close ties to Lorraine, while the House of d’Alsace has likewise remained historically connected to Metz and its surrounding region. Metz has long stood within the natural sphere of influence of Lorraine and remains closely bound to the fate of the Moselle lands.

Therefore, a coming conflict appears increasingly unavoidable as a necessary measure to restore order, security, and prosperity to the region.

events.fullText:

The Black Plague has struck Perm hard and in its midst many have chosen to flee to the edges of Great Realm in an attempt to escape this sickness. Wether these people have succeeded in evading the plague is unclear, but Komi settlement on the Upper Mozyn may nonetheless prove beneficial to Great Perm in the future.

events.fullText:
Disloyal tribes

A group constituting the majority of Mari and Chikyalik chieftains in Nema and Kasevo have ended their relationship with Perm, officially seceeding after a period of civil war among the Udmurts. While troubling and certainly surprising to the rest of the region, no action is immediately taken in retaliation against these southern peoples.

Emissaries from Cherdyn are quick to arrive to the region, but it is clear that these people have no intention to rejoin with Great Perm as during the strife in Udmurtia - they say - Perm did not do enough to secure their people and they suffered greatly from this conflict. Prince Yövgin' summons the chieftains and elders to Cherdyn, furious and raging, but he does not recieve their approval to march south with warriors in towe.
The reason is simple: the tribes are not yet ready, for after battling with the Ushkuyniks and attempting to reestablish order in Udmurtia - and then with the coming of the Great Plague the chieftains are unwilling in the slightest to lend their voice to such an action.

events.snippet:
# Archduke & Consul, Otto "the Merry" von Habsburg
While his older brother pursued glory, fame and greatness, Otto "the Merry" von Habsburg loyally ai
events.fullText:
# Archduke & Consul, Otto "the Merry" von Habsburg
While his older brother pursued glory, fame and greatness, Otto "the Merry" von Habsburg loyally aided his family's rise through other means. Everyone who met Otto knew he was a gregarious man (nicknamed "the Merry"), and he was able to get along with just about anyone. However, behind his smile and songs at the feasts, he held alterior motives, for with every friendship he curated, every connection he made, he would gain information and sway over those he deemed important. It was directly the work of Otto that the Habsburgs came to rule the Netherlands, with Otto himself serving as Consul of Holland. Through his machinations, he put himself as the prime candidate after the extinction of the d'Avesnes dynasty by leveraging his connections within the wealthy nobility of Rotterdam, Amsterdam and Den Haag. but his true test came AFTER he had secured his place as Consul, for he was a man from a distant land, and to the people of Holland, he had everything to prove. Yet, Otto was a man of many talents and one of those happened to be his silver tongue. For while his brother Albert studied history and numbers, Otto found a natural inclination for rhetoric. Otto could convince men to run into unbelievable odds, as he did the night before the battle of Mosbach. He could sway courtiers, dukes, soldiers, kings, mercenaries and even the commonfolk with his eloquent words and heartfelt speeches. Otto was also a pious man, and a devout believer in the restored Papacy and removal of the corrupt Orsini family, whom he had loathed the rise of. Now, Otto embarked for his greatest test, to secure the Habsburgs of the greatest prize in all of the Empire, the crown of Bohemia. He would plan to first visit the noble families of Bohemia, departing from his estate in Graz, starting with the well known z Rožmberka, who held vast power right across the Krumlov river bend.

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet:
# **Reconciliation and Reconstruction**

With the integration of Metz, stability gradually returns to the Moselle region after many years of uncertain
events.fullText:
# **Reconciliation and Reconstruction**

With the integration of Metz, stability gradually returns to the Moselle region after many years of uncertainty and unrest. Lorraine seeks not division, but reconciliation, and views this new chapter as an opportunity to restore peace and prosperity to the lands surrounding the Moselle.

As a sign of goodwill toward the city and its people, investments shall be made into the rebuilding and strengthening of Metz, as far as the hardships left behind by recent years and the plague allow. Efforts shall be taken to restore confidence, protect merchants and travellers, and encourage renewed prosperity for the remaining population.

Together with our allies in Trier, closer cooperation throughout the Moselle region shall help foster stability, commerce, and mutual prosperity. Through peace and careful governance, Lorraine hopes to contribute to a flourishing future for the region and its people.

**Written in the Year of Our Lord 1356**
**Raoul d’Alsace, Duke of Lorraine**

events.author: DerNette
events.snippet:
# **Rebuilding Prosperity in the Moselle Region**

As part of Lorraine’s continued reconstruction efforts, the establishment of a market in Metz is no
events.fullText:
# **Rebuilding Prosperity in the Moselle Region**

As part of Lorraine’s continued reconstruction efforts, the establishment of a market in Metz is now being planned as one of the first major investments into the region.

The hardships and consequences brought by the plague have shown us that dependence on large and distant markets may lead to shortages and uncertainty in times of crisis. Through its already established position and trade routes between the Kingdom of France and the Holy Roman Empire, Metz stands as an excellent location for renewed commerce and regional exchange.

The aim of this endeavour is to strengthen local security of supply, attract merchants back to the region after long years of hardship, and encourage the gradual resettlement and recovery of the city and its surrounding lands. Through careful investment and renewed stability, Lorraine hopes to restore prosperity to Metz and help it flourish once more.

As a secure and well-established city at the heart of the Moselle lands, Metz is well suited to serve as an economic centre for the region. Neighbouring rulers and regional authorities may be granted market privileges should they wish to participate and establish closer commercial ties.

**Written in the Year of Our Lord 1356**
**Raoul d’Alsace, Duke of Lorraine**

events.author: Olokun
events.snippet:
# The Wrath of House de Barcelona#

For generations, Sicily had stood beneath the banner of House de Barcelona. The bond between the Crown of Aragon a
events.fullText:
# The Wrath of House de Barcelona#

For generations, Sicily had stood beneath the banner of House de Barcelona. The bond between the Crown of Aragon and the island had not been forged through simple conquest, but through blood, struggle, and popular revolt. In the days of the Sicilian Vespers, the Sicilian people had risen against Angevin domination and called upon King Peter III de Barcelona of Aragon to defend their liberties and claims. House de Barcelona answered that call, driving out foreign oppression and establishing a dynasty that would rule Sicily for decades thereafter.
Since that time, Sicily had become a realm intrinsically tied to the prestige and legitimacy of the de Barcelona dynasty itself. To attack Sicily was to attack the blood and honor of the House de Barcelona.

But amidst the smoke of victory, fate turned unexpectedly.

Sultan Uthmar Abu Hafs Hafsid II of Tunisia, observing the conflict between the Christian powers, moved with extraordinary speed and cunning. While the great fleets clashed in the Strait and the attention of both Aragonese and Neapolitans remained fixed upon the sea, Hafsid forces landed upon Sicily itself. Under cover of darkness, Messina fell before many even realized Tunisian banners had reached the shore. By dawn, the island had slipped entirely from Sicilian control.

The court of Aragon was left stunned.

Though the Sultan permitted the de Barcelona family and loyal Sicilian nobility to flee the island unharmed, returning to Aragon embittered and dispossessed, the wound inflicted upon the Crown was profound. Sentiment toward Tunisia became conflicted within the halls of Barcelona and Saragossa alike. The Hafsids had shown restraint and even a measure of respect toward the dynasty, sparing the family and avoiding needless slaughter. Quietly, some admitted that Tunis had acted with more honor than Naples, Genoa, or even Rome itself.

Yet none could deny the truth: Sicily, ancient realm of the House of Barcelona, now rested beneath foreign rule.

The exiled Sicilian nobles carried with them stories of betrayal and abandonment. Across the Crown of Aragon, distrust toward the Italian states deepened into something darker and far more permanent. 
### Naples was condemned as the architect of the catastrophe. Genoa was reviled as a profiteering republic willing to strike at Sardinia while Christian blood was already being spilled. Ferrara was denounced for aiding an unjust dynastic war. Even the Papacy found itself increasingly distrusted, for Rome had abandoned Sicily despite solemn guarantees and shared faith. ###

From this calamity emerged a new consensus within the Crown: 
### The Italian powers could no longer be trusted to preserve either justice or stability within the Mediterranean ###

Rather than weakening Aragon, the loss of Sicily hardened it. The humiliation of the de Barcelona dynasty united nobles, merchants, and sailors alike behind calls for vengeance and recompense. Particular fury was directed toward Genoa, whose opportunism in Sardinia was now viewed as unforgivable. Throughout the Crown, demands grew louder: 
### Genoese possessions upon Sardinia be seized as rightful reparations for their aggression against House de Barcelona ###

In the royal court, whispers spread that this conflict had not ended with the fall of Sicily. Rather, it had only begun a new era of Mediterranean rivalry—one born from betrayal, dynastic humiliation, and the burning memory of a stolen kingdom.

And though Sicily had fallen, the House de Barcelona had not.

The dynasty endured.
The fleets endured.
And so too shall the bitterness of every peasant under the Crown of Aragon!

<@925830977291440210> <@170593650584059904> <@297185126947880960> <@315868897012219904> <@150411715757080576>

events.author: Onnirio
events.snippet:
**HANSETAG** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 
The Hansetag, the diet called in the Hanseatic Town Hall of Lübeck, has been attended by a great number
events.fullText:
**HANSETAG** <:hansaseal:1505589926673256659> 
The Hansetag, the diet called in the Hanseatic Town Hall of Lübeck, has been attended by a great number of aldermen or their representatives, though not by all the towns informally recognised as hanseatic; some, fearing reprisal from their sovereigns, have decided to stay out. 

Nevertheless, most of the attendees have agreed on matters of common treasury (by payment of a small fee to Hamburg, where the consul is actually seated), navy and defence, formalizing what has already been in force for at least two decades. The navy is planned to expand to at least 10 galleys and hanseatic cogs, <@1106651545296195655> ||which most have tacitly agreed to lend to Edward III should it be needed to protect the trade in the Channel||. There is also great expectation on using the treasury to expand memberships, which has now a clearer protocol<:Burghers:1139045024890368001> 

For the new members, first kontors must be built that ensure Hanseatic trade in the city. For that, the candidates must already have city rights and be vouched for current members, otherwise local nobility will be able to stop any kontors from being built. Ideally, they should not be capitals of a sovereign, as those are harder to deal with. If, after kontors are built there are no hostilities with local lords and trade flows (*level 2 kontor*), then the aldermen and council may agree to put forward a petition of membership in the next Hansetag.

There are some ideal candidates identified: Colchester, Boston (though it lacks rights, it’s a major wool port) & Oxford in England, Âbo & Stockholm in Sweden, Bergen in Norway, Grins in Frisia and Amsterdam, Dordrecth & Den Haag in Holland. 

Meanwhile, the expansion to Bordeaux has been slowed down due to the wars in the Channel, but Nantais in Brittany would work as a safe harbour against pirates and to promote trade in the Bay of Biscay.

events.snippet:
## To the Envoy of Tunis ## 


The Court of Aragon receives your presence and the words carried in the name of the Crown Prince Abu Bakr Hafsid with d
events.fullText:
## To the Envoy of Tunis ## 


The Court of Aragon receives your presence and the words carried in the name of the Crown Prince Abu Bakr Hafsid with due formality and attention.

His Majesty King Pere IV acknowledges the conduct of the forces of Tunis in Sicily, particularly the decision to preserve the lives, dignity, and holdings of those of the House of Barcelona who were present at the time of transition. Such restraint is noted.

However, the Kingdom of Aragon regards the situation in Sicily with continued concern. The island remains of deep dynastic and political significance to the Crown of Aragon, and recent events involving the intervention of Naples and its Genoese allies have further complicated an already fragile balance in the central Mediterranean.

Before any determination can be made regarding the present state of affairs, Aragon requires clarity on the intentions of the Sultanate of Tunis with respect to Sicily’s governance, the status of Catalan subjects therein, and the broader security of maritime trade routes in the region.

The King is willing to receive continued correspondence and dialogue on these matters in good faith. At present, Aragon withholds judgment and reserves its position until such clarity is achieved.

The envoy will be received with hospitality and permitted to present further words before the royal council.

By the will of the Crown,
Pere IV of Aragon

**To the City of Constantine & the True Emperor of Rome**


Following the recent conflict in Sicily, the Tunisian Sultanate became aware that new bann
events.fullText:
**To the City of Constantine & the True Emperor of Rome**


Following the recent conflict in Sicily, the Tunisian Sultanate became aware that new banners flew over the Island of Malta.  Merchants shared the news that the island had come under the governance of the Emperor of the Romans.  Not the Holy Roman Emperor, nor the Bishop of Rome, but the true Emperor of the Romans–the Emperor who ruled from Constantinople.

It was well known that there had been a schism among Christians that had divided the Catholic Latins from the Orthodox Greek, but news began to spread among the Sicilians of unrest stirred against the Orthodox church by the Christian clergy, and against the Emperor of the Romans.  Hearing this grave news, and envoy has made for the city of Constantinople, seeking audience within the court of the Byzantine Emperor.

Will the envoy be received?

<@302128861209165824>

events.author: Murlock
events.fullText:
**Pere Ordered to Reclaim Sicily <:rosary:898776109246726154> **
<@1007414347347271730> <@925830977291440210> <@297185126947880960> 

"You stand at risk of excommunication... and worse," spoke the Papal representative, having traveled across the Mediterranean to **Barcelona **to **demand** an audience.

"His Holiness is deeply disturbed by the ongoing conflict between the Barcelonan and Neapolitan courts. Both of your realms neglect the island of Sicily, allowing it to remain in heathen hands while Christian princes quarrel amongst themselves."

*The delegate is **Edmundus Filius Molloy**, an Irish Catholic who entered service to the Papacy four years prior for his resistance to Orsini influence. Known as an honest and just man, he has previously served in London under Papal authority. <@1106651545296195655>*

"Your responsibility to Christendom comes first, above all else, and above the personal ambitions of kings and princes. As such, by the authority of the Holy See, you are hereby formally **ordered to reclaim Sicily**. Rome has therefore proposed the following agreement:

The eastern side of the island shall be governed by the Neapolitans, while the western portion shall be entrusted to your Crown.

You, however, must fulfill your duty to the Church and act upon this command, reclaiming the island from Muslim rule. Should you refuse to recognize this decree, consequences shall follow, and the whole of Sicily shall instead be granted to the Neapolitans."

events.author: JarlFrosty
events.snippet:
# A Sister Needing Her Brother's Protection

With the defeat of the Royal Fleet of Naples, the Kingdom and her holdings are at a greater risk. The Kin
events.fullText:
# A Sister Needing Her Brother's Protection

With the defeat of the Royal Fleet of Naples, the Kingdom and her holdings are at a greater risk. The Kingdom of Serbia and the Byzantine Empire maintain claims on Albania and the Neapolitan Greek holdings; with the Kingdom no longer able to repel an invasion, the Queen has become worried that her enemies and those ambitious around her may attempt to strike the Kingdom. In order to ensure the protection of the Anjou Neapolitan holdings, Queen Giovanna writes to her late husband's brother, King Lajos of Hungary. 

||To the highly respected King Lajos of House Anjou, 

Brother, it has been some time since we have seen each other. It has been nearly eleven years since my beloved Andras, your brother, passed. Every day I miss his love, his voice, and his protection. Please be assured that I ensure your brother rests easy at a beautiful grave, fit for the loving King he was. 

While I wish we could see each other again and reunite over past memories of Andras, I fear I am unable to leave my Kingdom in these difficult times. In fact, I write to you because of these difficult times. As you may have already heard, I declared war on the false kingdom of Sicily and the Kingdom of Aragon with the aid of Genoa. We successfully invaded the island, but found that the Aragonese ignored Islamic invaders on the island to focus on our fleet, of which they nearly completely sunk. This allowed the island to fall into the barbaric hands of false prophet's followers, with millions of Christians being subject to oppressive rule. 

With the sinking of our royal fleet, I fear that the rejuvenated Byzantine Empire will see this as an opportunity to attack our holdings within Greece or Albania. The Duchy of Achaea and the Kingdom of Albania are unable to repel an attack without our support. Without a fleet, we can not send aid and they will be left defenseless. 

Brother, I call upon your aid against those who may attack us at this time. Whether it be the Orthodox heretics of the Byzantine Empire or the Serbian Kingdom, I hope I can rely on you to aid us in these times of trials and tribulations. 

Sincerely, 
Your Sister-in-Law, Giovanna de Anjou
Queen of the Kingdom of *Sicily* (Naples)||

<@423689694258397195>

events.author: ST Peter
events.snippet:
# The Humblest of Meetings.

After the successful conclusion of the meeting with John Palaiologos, Emperor of Constantinople, King Guy II of Cyprus an
events.fullText:
# The Humblest of Meetings.

After the successful conclusion of the meeting with John Palaiologos, Emperor of Constantinople, King Guy II of Cyprus and Jerusalem would prepare in the town of Paphos to depart westward. The most important of these stops was to be in Rome, so at last Guy could meet with Pope John XXIII, where he could pay reverence and submit to the holy father, and perhaps beseech his aid back east. So, before he departed on his long journey, Guy prepared a letter to be sent to that holy city.

Of course, what Guy couldn't possibly have known was that the greatest of all Bishops had probably already departed from the Eternal City. So, whoever would receive the letter would be left to deal with the newly arrived King. 

When opened, the letter adorned with the seal of the Lusignans would read:

*"To the revered Father and Lord John, High Pontiff by the grace of God, Pope of the Roman Church.

I have of late multiplied my supplications and prayers in the sight of God with joy and thanksgiving, since God has manifested his mercy by relieving my family of its greatest worry, and has given us our most desired wish: a blessed son, born on the most blessed of days. The Queen and King, both devout in our loving faith thank you in the name of our Lord Jesus for your aid in this matter of marriage. It has freed us from our tribulations, and our Christian brethren rejoice at the young Prince.

It is thus my most humblest of wishes to speak to you in person, blessed Father, to show my humility and reverence of the omnipotent God, and to give my submission and thanks personally. Whilst we are together we might also be well able to discuss how best to reclaim those most holy of lands, the promised land which all good Christians hold dear in their hearts and which is good to be fought for as long as one is draped in their faith.

I shall depart for Rome as soon as the winds and tide are abated.

--- Guy de Lusignan. By the grace of God, King of Cyprus and King of Jerusalem."*

-# King Guy II of Cyprus and Jerusalem

<@150411715757080576>

**(End)**

events.fullText:
# King Philippe VII de Valois the Greedy
Unlike his father, King Philippe VII is not as empathetic or fair. His greed is the most defining aspect of his character, which was seen in him canceling all the subsidies and gifts his father had left in his passing. Instead he wishes to keep the taxes his subjects pay hoarded in his coffers, exploit traitors and his enemies, and focus only on himself.

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
## Unsatiated lust of gold and silver
Creating his own image of being surrounded by gold and silver is all that matters, and nothing else. Such short
events.fullText:
## Unsatiated lust of gold and silver
Creating his own image of being surrounded by gold and silver is all that matters, and nothing else. Such short sightness could prove to be ruinous as time goes on.

### King Philippe VII methodology towards minting
Taxation of the estates is difficult. The nobility are far too powerful to tax greatly, the burghers are too few to support the Kingdom, and the peasantry too poor to sustain the Kingdom. Instead, the crown has relied upon the taxes of its subjects, like Normandy and Burgundy, to finance the crown’s expenditures. While lucrative, this just wasn’t satisfying enough for King Philippe VII who desired a more sturdy source of wealth. Which is why he implemented a ban upon exporting silver from the royal silver mine in Aulnay outside of France, and instead ordered its expansion to solely go towards the crown’s mint of the franc. This had improved his capacity to develop more wealth to finance his horde.

However, as minting proves to be very lucrative to King Phillippe VII, he may just let it get out of hand to amass immense piles of silver francs to feed his greed.

### The annexation of the County of Blois
First started by his secretive father King Phillippe VI who kept the matter private, the annexation of the county of Blois began as soon as Breton succession began. Doing so to better assert control over the city of Tours, and its closeness to Paris. He conducted diplomatic work, mostly bribing, with the region’s nobility to assert his authority to rule the lands directly while also promising the Chatillon family special privileges in the region if they conceded the lands to the King.

King Phillipe VI had done the bulk of the work, but King Phillippe VII would use his father’s private work to rush the process. || Having his agents develop a *legitimate* portfolio on Count Louis de Chatillon stretching the truth on the Count’s schemer activities privately financing enemies of King Phillippe VII. Documents not yet utilized until the moment King Phillippe VII holds certainty he can annex the lands without opposition by the nobility in Blois County.|| Doing so to better secure and control the road the silver of Aulnay travels to Paris on. A maneuver to protect his minting power, and horde bars of silver not made into coin in his treasury.

### The interest for a bloated treasury
What is the point of being wealthy if you cannot look at your riches? This is a serious problem King Phillippe VII wants to resolve, so he will cut back on financing infrastructure. Hoarding gold, silver, and gems in a grand treasury for him to enjoy. Truly a necessary decision for the betterment of the Kingdom ~~King Phillippe’s VII ego~~.

## None can wear the crown of France other than I
War between England and France remains, and with grave defeat at the fortress of Rannes no end is in sight. Thus far only two campaigns have occurred, but Brittany remains well under King Edward’s influence and King Edward still claims to be King of France. 

With things being as they are, there will be no peace in sight. Not by King Edward III, nor King Phillip VII.
-# (Battle posts not made during session)

### The Siege of Bordeaux
During King Philipe VI de Valois of France’s seizure of Gascony from England, the City of Bordeaux was a well fortified obstacle. The task of besieging, while initially begun by Sir Nigel of Corbie and done in bulk by French soldiers, was prestigiously given to Sir Bernhard Siuegmund von Brunshorn for his honor to serve Prince-Bishopric Baldwin of Trier beyond his death.

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
### The Battle of Arques
Once the City of Bordeaux had fallen, the armies of Philip VI and his allies went North to crush the main English army that h
events.fullText:
### The Battle of Arques
Once the City of Bordeaux had fallen, the armies of Philip VI and his allies went North to crush the main English army that had begun establishing a firm landing. However, no fortress had been claimed despite several unsuccessful and short sieges being  recorded. 

When the armies of France eventually arrived North, King Edward III ~~brilliantly~~ cowardly decided to make French terrain his own by hiding in dense woods rather than engaging in open battle. Doing so by setting up an encampment in Arques where his fleet could still supply him, where the forestry hid his many archers, and where the forestry would impede the mobility of the thousands of mounted nobility King Philip VI armies held.

Sir Nigel of Corbie recognized King Edward III's strategic move, but believed so thoroughly that even with such advantages he could manage to bring victory. It was then that the [sacrifice of the Mad 70](https://discordapp.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545331591610549/1508301666984919131)had had been made. This sacrifice burned through some of the ammo of the dishonorable longbowmen of England, and provided essential time needed for the disorganized levies and noblemen of France to move to the undermanned center. A dangerous development swiftly resolved within an hour of battle.

The disorganized French army only kept together by Sir Nigel command barely won the Battle of Arques, but at devastating cost to the noble cavalry who could barely manage to move in the woods. Often being out maneuvered by English spearmen and ambushed by bands of longbowmen. Very dishonorable English tactics, but very deadly. Eventually the English would run out of land to flee and maneuver, and only one of their  flanks remained under devastating assault. King Edward III had then made another ~~strategic~~ cowardly decision, which was to retreat to his Northern occupations and reduce his losses.

However, Burgundian armies would later destroy King Edward III armies in Flanders several times over.

events.author: Cloud
events.snippet:
### The Second Breton Campaign
Where his father failed, he will aim to succeed. Not for prestige or entirely to restore his crown’s borders, but inste
events.fullText:
### The Second Breton Campaign
Where his father failed, he will aim to succeed. Not for prestige or entirely to restore his crown’s borders, but instead for his greed. 

Thus, he prepares to wage the Second Campaign for Brittany. However, if he were to succeed, there is no real certainty it would go to the Montforts. The lands could instead be entirely pillaged and left burning in the hands of King Edward III, maybe held as bargaining tools to empty English coffers, or even outright seized (annexed) and thoroughly taxed.

While King Phillippe VII's aim is not quite clear due to his greedy nature, it is very clear he will devastate the Breton lands in the upcoming conflict where he will employ the skills of the Black Eagle of Brittany who swears his loyalty to the French crown. Not only that, but King Philippe VII seeks to challenge King Edward III at sea to deal raids upon English shores. Thus, he offers 350 gold to the Doge of Genoa <@170593650584059904>  to hire its entire warfleet to use as mercenaries against England, and a reward of 400 more if victory is found.
-# war dec May 5th
-# Those certainly involved <@1106651545296195655> <@255439449448644608>. Those that would be interested in being involved <@170593650584059904> <@696548460094029914> <@259426601865838592>.

events.author: Redcola
events.snippet:
**The Truce of Saint-Pierre and Brittany alone** 

The Breton War of Succession would be officially paused by the signing of a church meditated truce,
events.fullText:
**The Truce of Saint-Pierre and Brittany alone** 

The Breton War of Succession would be officially paused by the signing of a church meditated truce, though not a full peace treaty as both Jeanne de Clisson's piracy campaign and Jeanne de Montfort’s continued claims of her infant son kept the matter open the French crown could in the wake of the plague no longer continue the campaign lest risk revolts, wars on other borders or else. The uneasy peace would in the region counterbalance England's Loss of Aquitaine with the admission of Brittany as in the English sphere of influence and unlikely to change while the issue of succession remains open. The status qou created by the Dreux dynasty is finally dead, in its place Brittany is changed. 

The great bishops of the realm are now in conflict with the growing reformist Breton monastic orders whose theology has steadily shifted, merging Celtic mysticism with fatalistic philosophies spawned from the war. Superstition now becoming rife in the interior. 

Culturally wider still for the first time in hundreds of years the majority spoken tongue is Breton not Gallo, the eastern french speakers devastated from the black death while the western Bretons remained untouched. Combined with such dramatic time period and so much shifting of traditional class and gender roles has invited in a second age of Breton culture, inspired by the grand tales of the Breton Heroes and of a growing interest in the language again, though the nobility remain firmly French culturally their politics now shifts.

Now within the English sphere of influence political loyalties now remain in a eery quagmire, most major houses owning land in France losing them to the crown, while those owning in England now gain primacy and support, not least the Duchess Janned who retains the Earldom of Richmond. While the shadow of king Phillip VII hangs over them all, enforcing a unity of sorts if only because those who swapped sides so many times now grow uncertain of how much mercy they can expect.  

**The Ankou waits, ready to pile his cart for honour is dead and peace is a distant memory  **

events.snippet:
**Polish-Lithuanian War**

The papal mediated talks between the Teutonic Order and the Kingdom of Poland resulted in a most unusual conclusion - that
events.fullText:
**Polish-Lithuanian War**

The papal mediated talks between the Teutonic Order and the Kingdom of Poland resulted in a most unusual conclusion - that the Papacy recognise King Casimir III of Poland as the Grand Duke of Lithuania in exchange for relinquishing claims to Pomeralia and other territories. The Polish King would be slow to enforce this claim as Polish-Lithuanian ties remained deep - but the power of the clergy and calls for a firmer anti-Teutonic stance persuaded him to launch war in 1444. 

The Polish-Lithuanian War would last three years, with Polish forces moving through the southern Brest region into Lithuania proper. The greatest engagement came at the Battle of Grodno where a Polish Army defeated their Lithuanian counterparts, enabling them to march on Vilnius itself and sack the city. 

A peace treaty was signed wherein Poland seized Brest, Sudovia, Dainava, Vilnius and Samogitia with the Grand Duchy pushed back to Aukštaitija north of the Neman. The new Polish administration gave broad autonomy to the Lithunian nobility provided they convert to Catholicism which in practice has meant a steady trickle of Polish nobles and administrators taking control of the region. But as the Polish forces came home they took with them another ailment...

The Pope has arrived in Mainz and
events.fullText:
**Viriliter Agite <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2kMWG6OC0C4
-# @here

The Pope has arrived in Mainz and has requested the presence of bishops, cardinals, archbishops, and men of influence to travel and honor the presence of the Vicar of Christ. Pope Ioannes XIII is the man who shattered Orsini influence and forged a renewed Church founded upon the values of scripture and the **mercy of Christ**<:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> . He believes firmly in the **primacy of the Papal office**<:battlepope:1139595789107204286> , and walks across the Empire with the authority and bearing of one who sees himself as shepherd of all Christendom, **as though he were Emperor in all but name. <:AlbertTheGreat:1118804666180976670> **

Here, he welcomes all to speak and resolve disputes, be they matters of marriage, territorial claims, or guidance in the wake of the terrible Black Death <a:d_ratJAM:837315629870219307> . He is present **as a servant of God**, willing to bring the holiness and mercy of Christ **to all who seek such counsel. <:sacredheart:1113686535804555304> **

After his address, he makes himself available **for any and all meetings. **He is here to help. The Church is here, and wishes to embrace all the faithful beneath the grace of Christ. Let no man fear to speak, **for Holy Mother Church stands ready to guide, reconcile, and heal the wounds of Christendom. <:poinsot:1126591652400742502> **

> "Holy servants of God, a challenge had emerged from the darkest pits of Hell itself. Satan and the serpent walked among us|| <a:AmongUs:841889157495128064>|| . Towns have been abandoned, cities cracked apart by the decay of the old world, and rats still plague our fields even now <:Grain:1141431046563430504> .
> 
> Yet I ask you this: do not blame the Jew, nor your neighbor. Do not turn your hatred upon the weak or fearful. Instead, look first to the Church, for in truth, we too have failed you <:rose:865579087883272192><:mary3:1126382749612249108>  .
> 
> The Black Death <a:d_ratJAM:837315629870219307> , this great scourge of Europe, was born from corruption, pride, and sinful ambition within Christendom itself. The dealings of the Orsini and those who enabled them brought darkness upon the Church in a time when the faithful most needed the light of Christ. No physician nor leech could cure such a plague of the soul. <:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> 
> 
> But through your faith, through your support, and through the resolve of the Empire and the faithful, that corruption was exposed. We, the servants of God, endured His trial and prevailed against the wickedness that sought to consume His Church.
> 
> God tested us. He tested our unity, our mercy, our resolve, and our devotion. And how did Christendom answer?
> 
> We defeated Satan. We endured. **We prevailed. <:sacredheart:1113686535804555304> **
> 
> Now the Church stands renewed, cleansed through suffering and repentance. **Pain was our Salvation.** And today, gathered here together beneath Heaven, we may begin reconciliation through prayer, through mercy, and through faith in Christ our Lord <:AveChristusRex:836370696904114186> .
> 
> For the Kingdom of God is not some distant dream alone beyond death. **It is the kingdom we are called to build here upon this Earth**: a Christendom united in faith, guided by mercy, protected by justice, and strengthened together as one holy people beneath **God** and by the grace of the mother <:mary3:1126382749612249108> .
> 
> Therefore we all Viriliter Agite (Act courageously), to ensure the Orsini never happen again. To Prevent another Black Death, and to ensure the glory of our Empire. All of you must be actors in our Kingdom for the grace of god. Be vigilant. Be courageous. **Be Faithful <:handcross:639855343618228254> **"
