events.snippet:
# The efforts of the Dubois Bishops.

The faith of the Greek serfs(paroikoi) has never been a concern for the Lusignan Kings of Cyprus. The serfs were
events.fullText:
# The efforts of the Dubois Bishops.

The faith of the Greek serfs(paroikoi) has never been a concern for the Lusignan Kings of Cyprus. The serfs were made simply to ensure the Kingdom's food never ran a deficit and to serve in the army when it conducted its small-time raids on the mainland. In 1337, Prince Guy(now King) even promised the Emperor Andronikos III that he and his father(Hugh IV) had no intention of converting those who followed the Greek Rite, as long as church property remained owned by the Roman Catholic Dioceses of Cyprus. It was the truth, even as the faith of the island would begin to shift in the following decades.

For even if the Lusignan's cared not for the faith of the Cypriots, the Latin clergy was another matter. In 1349 Peter Dubois was made the new Bishop of Limassol and sent from the west to take his new ecclesiastical seat on Cyprus. Just two years later in 1351 his cousin William Dubois was made the new Archbishop of Nicosie, and he too came to Cyprus. Together, the two Dubois made significant efforts, more than any other in their positions before them, to convert the native Cypriots. To the countryside around the capital of Nicosie they went, as the urban cities on Cyprus were already dominated by Catholic believers.

It took years upon years of effort to accomplish anything at all, but by 1356 they did indeed have something to show for all the work they had put in. At first the conversions were in the hundreds, before turning into the thousands. There was certainly no Roman Catholic majority around Nicosie, not even close, but the minority has grown significantly. When compared to the lands around the cities of Famagusta or Larnaca, the native Cypriot serfs living around Nicosie stands alone for its amount of Latin Rite followers. It is a remarkable accomplishment to even get this far, and the Archbishop William Dubois and the Bishop Peter Dubois are to thank for it...

-# (Left to Right) the Bishop of Limassol, the Archbishop of Nicosie, the new Catholic Cypriot serfs.

**(End)**

# A Question of Legitimacy 

When Queen Giovanna agreed to marry the Count of Finale, Carl del Carretto in a matrilineal marriage, there was no knowle
events.fullText:
# A Question of Legitimacy 

When Queen Giovanna agreed to marry the Count of Finale, Carl del Carretto in a matrilineal marriage, there was no knowledge of the Count having any children. However, within two to three years of the couple being married, a "son" of the Count appeared, Settimio "del Carretto". The boy was born out of wedlock when the Count was 16, with no record of a mother. Some suspected the Count impregnated a brothel wench but it didn't matter, had the Queen known of the child before the marriage, she would've never married the Count. Regardless of her anger, the Count was the father of her children and his titles should be inherited his legitimate sons. 

Before reaching out to Rome over this matter, the Queen consulted the Cardinal of Naples to question the legitimacy based on the facts. Because of the legitimacy of Settimio can not be proven, Cardinal Andrea Esposito can not confirm the legitimacy of the child. 

With this, the letter has been sent to the Pope to request the Vicar of Christ's judgement; 

|| Holy Father, 

The Queen of Naples has brought to my attention the possibility of her husband, Count Carl del Carretto, of having a child born out of wedlock, around the age of 16, a few years prior to marrying her. The child in question is Settimio del Carretto, who is apparently the firstborn child of Carl. However, upon questioning the child and various family members, there seems to be know knowledge or record of the mother. In addition, there is no record that shows Carl has been previously married.  

I call upon your judgement as Vicar of Christ to investigate this matter and to delegitimize Settimio del Carretto, stripping him of being heir to the Count of Finale, instead bestowing the inheritance upon the Crown Prince of Naples, Giorgio de Anjou. 

God Bless, 
Cardinal Anrea Esposito ||

events.snippet:
## *Hodierna Electio*

Before Karel’s body had even been delivered to Praha from Frankfurt, the estates met to discuss his successor. Jan was lucky to
events.fullText:
## *Hodierna Electio*

Before Karel’s body had even been delivered to Praha from Frankfurt, the estates met to discuss his successor. Jan was lucky to have died in his forties with an adult son, but Karel’s untimely death in 1348 left no such adult son. That meant the throne was open for election at the basest level, as no potential contender immediately drew enough support among the estates to be hastily elected. Three were considered, and only one could obtain the crown.

**Jan Jindřich**, younger brother to Karel, a man whose legacy as the Count of Tyrol expelled from his own lands by his wife followed him ceaselessly, even into his marriage with **Elżbieta Piast**, eldest daughter of **Kazimierz III** of Poland. He was the closest in blood to Karel, and widely considered a sufficiently weak and pliable monarch: but the odds of him bringing the kingdom into war with Poland and Hungary stirred disagreement among his supporters.

**Johann von Wittelsbach**, the Duke of Lower Bavaria, sired to the marriage between **Heinrich XIV** and **Markéta z Lucembursko**, Karel’s older sister. His support emerged from his Wittelsbach blood, the ties already sown between the Wittelsbachs and Luxembourgs under Karel’s reign, with his Luxembourg mother providing the necessary means of candidacy. This push would ultimately fail as the estates feared the absorption of Bohemia into the sprawling Wittelsbach house, with some viewing Johann as too-German and too liable to being merely an agent of Kaiser Stephan’s will, and his alone.

**Otto “the Merry” von Habsburg**, the dark horse candidate of sorts, whose marriage to Karel’s younger sister **Hana** in 1335 provided him the introduction to the Bohemian aristocracy he would need fourteen years later. Classically, Hana would have went to Austria, not Otto to Bohemia, but their marriage took place in the Moravian city of Znojmo, and the correspondence between Hana and her family kept Otto’s names and deeds relevant in the circles of Praha which enabled the pivotal context that he would come to rely upon. Ultimately his election would come about on his promises of ecclesiastical independence for the Bohemian dioceses, in light of his brother **Albrecht II**’s known cavorting with Rome, and for the prospects of military guarantee against Poland and Hungary should war break out and dissolve the Viségrad agreement. Perhaps most importantly across the gathering of a sufficient party to see through his election was his ties to Haus Luxemburg, which placed him as a crucial cooling factor between them and Haus Habsburg, and enabled him to be elected without it being internalized as a deposition of Luxembourg rule within Bohemia.

Despite the [fierce parting words](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1508269112273408030/1509131593002782730) of Rudolf II, Count-Palatine, the Wittelsbach party seemingly accepted their defeat—for now—and withdrew from Praha without calling up arms in pursuit of Johann von Wittelsbach’s claims. At the head of the victorious party, Otto *der Fröhliche* proceeded to Vyšehrad, the historic royal seat of past Bohemian kings, where he bowed before the bast shoes and satchel of **Přemsyl the Ploughman**, the mythic progenitor of the Přemsylid dynasty. A holy mass was held in the Basilica of St. Peter and Paul, and a procession encompassing the breadth of the new royal household and its noble entourage occurred throughout the city, taking them to the city center and back to the royal castle. Within the St. Vitus Cathedral the coronation was undertaken in all its parts, led by the **Archbishop of Mainz**, **Henrik II**: the anointing of King Otto, like David and like the Kings of France, the handing over of the Royal Jewels, and the consecration of the crown, followed by a mass and Otto taking his oath before the throne, the nobles, and the people outside.

Following his coronation was that of his wife, the new Queen of Bohemia, **Hana z Lucembursko**. At the conclusion of their coronations and associated rituals, which had already taken up nearly two days, King Otto led the festive atmosphere into the weekend with a protracted period of celebrations: a royal banquet was held, with portions of the expenses directed to the commoners in attendance, and ending off with a tourney. Otto *der Fröhliche*, the Merry, known for his joyous court celebrations, ensured his introduction to the people of Bohemia was well-fitting for his name, and that he took the opportunity to lavish the realm with his generosity in a display of royal affinity that would carry with him for his reign.

In the name of Almighty God, eternal and sovereign, let it be known to all princes, lords, and faithful peoples:

That betwee
events.fullText:
**The Treaty of Brasso**
In the name of Almighty God, eternal and sovereign, let it be known to all princes, lords, and faithful peoples:

That between the Kingdom of Hungary and the Duchy of Wallachia, an accord of peace and concord has been lawfully concluded.

Firstly, the Duke of Wallachia, together with his lands and subjects, does humbly and willingly accept the one true and apostolic faith of the Holy Church, renouncing all former errors and heresies, and placing himself under the guidance and protection of the Catholic faith.

In return for this righteous and pious act, the King of Hungary does solemnly recognize and affirm the rightful dominion of Wallachia over the lands of Moldavia, acknowledging its authority and governance therein without contest.

Furthermore, both parties agree to lay down all hostilities, grievances, and claims of war between them, and to uphold peace, mutual respect, and good faith henceforth.

Let this accord be witnessed and sealed in Brasso, in the year of Our Lord 1355, under the eyes of God and men alike.

*Signed,*
<@423689694258397195> King of Hungary
<@493830084893999104> Duke of Wallachia

events.snippet:
# La Serenissima Venezia
The year is 1356, and Venice rises from the water like a vision carved from marble and salt.

At dawn, the lagoon is silver b
events.fullText:
# La Serenissima Venezia
The year is 1356, and Venice rises from the water like a vision carved from marble and salt.

At dawn, the lagoon is silver beneath a pale Adriatic sky. Bells ring across the islands, from parish towers, monastery cloisters, and the great basilica of San Marco, their echoes mingling with the cries of gulls and the slap of oars against dark canals. Merchants in wool cloaks hurry along narrow fondamenta while gondoliers weave between cargo barges heavy with pepper, silk, wax, and grain. The scent of seawater mixes with incense, fish markets, and eastern spices brought from ports half a world away.

This is the Republic of Venice at the height of its confidence: wealthy, disciplined, and fiercely proud. For centuries the city has called itself *La Serenissima* , the Most Serene Republic, though beneath the polished rituals lies constant tension. The ruler of Venice, the Doge Marco Dandolo, lives in splendor within the newly expanded Doge's Palace. Yet by 1356 he is less a king than the ceremonial face of an intricate oligarchy. Real power belongs to the patrician families whose names dominate the Great Council: the Dandolo, Contarini, Morosini, and Gradenigo. Their sons command galleys, negotiate treaties, and oversee commerce from Constantinople to the Black Sea. Every important decision is watched by councils, committees, and magistrates designed to prevent tyranny. And there is reason for caution. 
Only a year earlier, the city survived political trauma after the failed coup of Doge Marin Faliero, who in 1355 was executed for conspiring against the Venetian nobility. His fate still hangs over the republic like a warning bell.

[Date:1356-01-05][Country:Venice][Location:Venice][Tag:History]
# Laguna Argentata
Beyond the lagoon, the wider world is unsettled. The Black Death has recently ravaged Europe, carrying away perhaps half the population in some regions. Venice itself suffered terribly when plague ships entered the harbor in 1348. Empty houses still stand in forgotten corners of the city, and priests still recite masses for entire vanished families. Yet commerce resumes. It always resumes. Venetian merchants calculate profit even amid catastrophe.

To the east, the republic watches the slow weakening of the Byzantine Empire and the rise of Turkish powers in Anatolia. To the west looms its bitter maritime rival, *la Repubblica Genovese*. Their struggle for dominance over Mediterranean trade is relentless, fought with convoys, spies, sabotage, and sudden naval war.

Yet daily life continues with astonishing vitality. Artisans hammer bronze in crowded workshops, glassmakers on Murano guard their secrets jealously, scribes illuminate manuscripts under candlelight, pilgrims departing for the Holy Land crowd the quays beside mercenaries seeking contracts abroad. In the Arsenal, the vast state shipyard that amazes foreign visitors, laborers can construct war galleys with almost industrial efficiency, contributing to the ever-growing navy of the Republic.

# ANNA VON WITTELSBACH "THE PRIZE" HAS MADE HER DECISION: A NEW HUSBAND HAS BEEN CHOSEN AND THE MARRIAGE CEREMONY BEGINS! # 

Open to the public, right at the heart of Heidelberg inside of its temple: The marriage of the new Heir of the Palatinate of the Rhine was announced and celebrated. 

Anna von Wittelsbach "the Prize" is officially married to Prince Count of Bavaria-Chiemgau  Albrecht von Wittelsbach, son of the late Kaiser Ludwig IV and brother to the current Emperor of the Holy Roman Empire.

The celebrations were made and the whole of the City cheered on to see that the House of Wittelsbach was truly united, now with two abled members of the dynasty set to succeed in the future.

The unity of the House became ever more apparent with the second treaty of Pavia. With the Wittelsbachs united, the question now remained of the future. But reports do show us that this new Marriage will likely be a happy one, Lady Anna has been seen having her cheeks brush with her new tall and capable husband. 

For Prince Albrecht, he also seems happy, for this union certainly secures him yet another title to rule.

events.author: Reehaw
events.snippet:
## *Chlapec-Král*
-# The Boy King

-# Above image was rendered using AI.

Four years ago he was elected, and yet now his body returned to the same cat
events.fullText:
## *Chlapec-Král*
-# The Boy King

-# Above image was rendered using AI.

Four years ago he was elected, and yet now his body returned to the same cathedral in which he was coronated. Otto *der Fröhliche* von Habsburg, King of Bohemia, having died in Wien, was returned to be laid to rest in the royal crypt of the St. Vitus Cathedral. There, he was entombed alongside Karel, his predecessor, and his dynastic forebearer Rudolf, son to Kaiser Albrecht I. 

Otto’s death brought his reign, naturally, to its end. His son with Hana z Lucembursko was too young, an infant, and their daughters were lower in consideration and reserved thus for when no suitable male heir could be found. Fortunately, there was one, who the estates could readily rally behind and prevent a fraught and contentious election.

Jan z Lucembursko, Johann von Luxembourg, Jang vu Lëtzebuerg, Jean de Luxembourg, all names from languages which he spoke, which he had learned attending the Parisian Court in his youth. It was shortly after his father’s victory at Mosbach, which left Karel disfigured, that he would send young Jan to France to stay under the protection of his brother-in-law, King **Philippe VI**.  While Karel involved himself in trying to restore order and authority in the empire following Ludwig IV’s death, Jan was raised like he was in, in all the lavish trappings of the French Court and its embellished centralized monarchy which employed the grandest bureaucracy in all of Europe. When his father died in Frankfurt in 1348, he was almost whisked away back to Praha before the arrival of messengers from his mother, Blanche de Valois, in Olomouc described the estates had decided his youth prevented his election. So his caretakers and tutors kept him in Paris, beyond the visit to Luxembourg to be crowned as its count.

While in Paris, his primary tutor was the Francien clergyman **Alain Richard**, who was born in the County of Cephalonia off the coasts of Greece, in the remnants of *Frankokratia*. His excellent tutoring provides much of the finer studies which Jan excelled at in his youth, and eventually came to receive a delegation from King **Otto** *der Fröhliche* **von Habsburg**, his father’s successor, bearing news of a slight stipend from Bohemia to supplement his expenses in the West as a gift “among the houses.” Under Richard’s education he grew up pious, and into his teenage years carried a formative devotion to the Church, even as he attended tournaments in France and Luxembourg. For a time as the *Magna Mortalitas* ravaged Europe he and his household were kept in the castle of Luxembourg itself, where it was hoped much like failed besiegers before, not even the plague could surmount its defenses.

As Jan departed from his teacher, Alain, and went to Bohemia for his election and subsequent coronation, he carried physical forms of the most impactful literature bestowed upon him: Aquinas’ *De Regno*, and Aegidius’ *De Regimine Principum*. 

Heeding the advice offered to him in memory of Otto’s rule, and yet still succumbing to his nature as effectively a foreign newcomer, Jan built his government cautiously after receiving and swearing upon the Inaugural Diplomas. **Jindřich z Rožmberka** was made **Supreme Marshal**, head of the Bohemian Diet, for his natural sway over much of the nobility. To have done otherwise so soon would have suggested an insult. Yet to maintain peace among the houses, he named **Jindřich z Dubé** as **Supreme Burgrave**, the effective vice-regent and chief office second only to the king himself. The first delegated task he bestowed upon z Dubé was to continue his efforts in revitalizing Praha, as he had done so beneath Karel and Otto, giving him renewed royal backing in negotiating with the town council. 

As Jan arrived in Bohemia unwed, he would build from his older sister **Markéta**’s marriage to the Prince-Elector of Meissen, **Friedrich III von Wettin**, and marry **Maria Magdalena von Hohenzollern**, the second daughter to the Count of Ansbach, **Johann II** *der Erweber* **von Hohenzollern**, who would soon become his closest advisor and paternal authority as his father-in-law within his court and household in Bohemia. 

King Jan II, merely sixteen, is brother-in-law to the Elector of Meissen, and first cousin to the King of France.

<@222439858927435776> <@763652399784919080> <@190640828954181632> <@215639063502258176>

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
[The succession issue ](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1509335054072025139)

The power of the crown declined unde
events.fullText:
[The succession issue ](https://discord.com/channels/507915886477312023/1505545492736639056/1509335054072025139)

The power of the crown declined under Casimir III as he focused on foreign affairs but has collapsed following his death. In his place have emerged the szlachta who have largely governed the country without issue.  

However the Greater Poland Uprising has led to the dynamic to shift. The attempt to crown a Piast king led to an unusual alliance between the pro-Luxembourg and pro-Angevin factions with the defeat of the Piasts now laying the groundwork for succession. But each candidate has flaws...

The first is Louis of Hungary. By the terms of the second Congress of Visegrád he is the rightful king but there has been deep reluctance to enable this. Thus far the Polish-Hungarian alliance has stagnated and the Hungarian crowns hostility to Bohemia has unnerved some who want peace towards their western neighbour. More obviously however is Louis personality - he is widely regarded as both cruel and a babbling buffoon which has made him unattractive to the szlachta. Furthermore there is a concern of a Polish-Hungarian union becoming long lasting and domineering. Thus there is mind towards carving out further concessions for his succession. 

The second is Elizabeth of Poland. Whilst there was worry of a female ruler during her viceregal reign she has proved to be amenable to the demands of the szlachta. However her marriage to Jan Henry of Bohemia complicates matters - Jan Henry is disliked for his arrogance and his own manoveruings for the Bohemian throne means another potentially overbearing union is possible. 

Finally there is Princess Kunegunda who would almost certainly reign jure uxoris with her husband, Stephen. Whilst the least legitimate choice this has been mooted as a potential compromise, keeping the throne to Anjou but through the Piast line.

The power of the szlachta is strong but they are bribable. Key concerns for any candidate would be 
-securing alliances against Poland's enemies
-retaining the primacy of the Polish crown

events.title: 1356 Polish royal election

events.author: HK je Srbija
events.snippet:
**The Coronation of Louis of Hungary and the Angevin Articles **

After weeks of deliberation the sejm elects King Louis of Hungary as King of Poland,
events.fullText:
**The Coronation of Louis of Hungary and the Angevin Articles **

After weeks of deliberation the sejm elects King Louis of Hungary as King of Poland, fulfilling the Congress of Visegrad and securing the Polish throne to the House of Anjou. There is celebrations in the streets over the succession being resolved, although many privately fear Hungarian domination of the kingdom. 

Many of these fears are allayed with the Angevin Articles, a serires of stipulations the new king pledges to uphold. These articles consolidate the idea of the Polish Crown being a seperate legal entity, a concept already well established in Bohemia and beginning its nucleus in Hungary. The Articles are as follows - 

The king is to call a sejm every four years of six weeks, of which alone can set the create new taxes, tariffs or such
That the Crown of Poland (consisting of the Kingdom of Poland, the Grand Duchy of Lithuania and other spheres) are indivisible and and inseparable
That the rights and privileges of the Crown of Poland be unaltered and unmolested
That the successors to King Louis as king of Poland have court and reign in Krakow; if inheritance betwixt the two kingdoms is to be divided resultingly then it henceforth should remain in the House of Anjou through the line of either King Louis of Hungary or Prince Stephen of Hungary. 
That there shalt not be an alliance between the Kingdom of Hungary and those that threaten Poland; namely, the states of the Tatar Yoke and the State of the Teutonic Order

The succession has not been confirmed but it is likely that Stephen of Hungary, Louis brother, is *de facto* heir until Louis's infant son Robert is near the age of majority.

events.author: DinoHunter65100
events.snippet:
# Two Kingdoms; One King - The Regna Andegavensis #
After the Polish Sejm came to the righteous conclusion of electing King Louis I de Anjou, the resu
events.fullText:
# Two Kingdoms; One King - The Regna Andegavensis #
After the Polish Sejm came to the righteous conclusion of electing King Louis I de Anjou, the resulting diplomatic development has seen the two great eastern Catholic kingdoms united under one monarch. From Lithuania to Croatia, all must pass through the Angevin realms to travel between East and West. The shield of Christdom has expanded and now a Catholic realm spans from the Baltic to the Adriatic Sea, protecting all in the west the dangers of the east. Although there are concerns about future conflicts resulting from the Articuli Andegavensis, for the time being the two crowns have been united under one king. 

## Long may the the Regna Andegavensis Endure ##

events.fullText:
# ALL FAR TOO REAL AROUND THE NUMBING

||In secret, a Papal representative of some notoriety meets with an emissary of the Karamanid Bey within the Cypersian city of Kyrenia. Within the meeting, several key terms are agreed upon as follows:||

||-The Karamanids Beylik will not impede Catholic efforts against the Mamlukes.||

||-5 ducats of gold shall make their way from papal coffers unto the hands of the Karamanid Bey a month.||

||All money and aid received from the Church shall go towards opposing the Byzantine Empire and building a Turkic State (the Karamanids) to fight them.||

||-There shall be no conflict between the Knights of Rhodes and The Karamanid Bey, and they work together in order to oppose the Byzantines.||

events.author: jackhearts
events.snippet: The negotiations between the Papal States, Knights of St John and the Byzantine Empire have concluded in the following treaty, named for the location
events.fullText:
The negotiations between the Papal States, Knights of St John and the Byzantine Empire have concluded in the following treaty, named for the location of the Crusader Host set to invade the Byzantine Empire.

**Treaty of Hierapolis**
BISHOPRIC OF MÉTRAI
The Papal States shall establish a Bishopric at Métrai, near Constantinople. This arrangement shall be subject to review every fifty years, that the harmony between the Roman and Orthodox faiths may be continuously assured.

PROTECTION OF CATHOLIC MISSIONS
The Byzantine Empire shall permit and protect Catholic missions throughout its lands, contingent upon the Holy See providing sponsorship to the Emperor for that purpose.

RESTORATION OF BYZANTINE LANDS
Should Catholic powers render assistance, whether political, economic, or military, in the restoration of Byzantine territories including Macedonia, Albania, Thrace, Morea, West Anatolia, and the Aegean Archipelago, the Empire shall in return permit the establishment of Catholic preference Fiefdoms within those restored lands.

THE ANATOLIAN LEASE
The lands of Anatolia liberated from the Turk by the Knights of Saint John shall be held in lease by the Order as a necessary means toward the Liberation of the Levant. Upon the completion of that sacred charge, said lands shall be returned in full to the Byzantine Empire. This the Order swears upon the honour of Saint John.

PLEDGE OF BYZANTINE STEEL
The Byzantine Empire pledges its banners and military assistance to the Liberation of the Levant, should the call come, in recognition of the shared Christian duty to restore the Holy Land to the faithful.

events.snippet:
## A steady agreement on rocky ground

The people of Armagnac wirthe and toil in the dirt, their homes in ruin, their parents and children sick, their
events.fullText:
## A steady agreement on rocky ground

The people of Armagnac wirthe and toil in the dirt, their homes in ruin, their parents and children sick, their crops devasted. The remnants of the Aquitaine campaign and the sweep unknown plague across the lands have left the people of Gascony more resentful and wary of the nobility in Northern France.

Count Centule IV, having taken the seat through lineage claims on his mother after deposing the bastard hunchback heir of Joan d'Armagnac, sends a messenger to the King; a last ditch attempt to alleviate the woes and suffering of his people.

For years after the Aquitaine Campaign, the county holdings of Foix in Marsan have resulted in high toll taxing and general raiding of merchants and traders passing from the Capital Castle in Nogaro to the port city of Bayonne.

Count Centule, reminding him of the loss of life and heading of the campaign completed in the South of France, asks for the Foix lineage to be ridiculed for their crimes against his traders and for their holdings to be released to him. In exchange, Centule will release Rodez and Lautrec in the southern hills to the Foix dynasty as a means of appeasement, despite the suffering of his people at the hands of the Foix dynasty's inaction and inability to rule. <@460814348487753730>

events.author: Bear
events.snippet:
## A New Count takes the Throne

Centule IV of Astarac seeks his rightful claim to the county Armagnac through his mother. His maternal ancestory, Ber
events.fullText:
## A New Count takes the Throne

Centule IV of Astarac seeks his rightful claim to the county Armagnac through his mother. His maternal ancestory, Bernard V, promised his mother that their lineage would maintain the rights to the county of Armagnac. However, French Nobility interreference lost the Astarac's the seat in Astarac and their legitimate dynastic claim to Armagnac.

Angered by this, Centule IV, the wise young man he was, capitalized on the untimely death of Joan d'Armagnac during the unknown plague and the power scramble that ensued afterwards to make a play on the seat. 

Nobility in Armagnac were upset the hunchback first son rose to the seat of power with his father's death, much favoring the younger, more handsome, more talented son. As such, opportunities for a coupe manifested more easily than Centule IV had planned.

Under the darkness of the new moon night, Centule IV marched on the county castle with supporting noblemen and deposed the hunchback son of his control of Armagnac. Much is not known of the hunchback after he was dragged from the castle, but no one heard from him again.

As for the remaining lineage of d'Armagnac, his other offspring were locked up, if they were sons or challengers to the seat, or married off to surrounding Gascon or French nobles, if they were women. As such, Centule IV took control of the seat of county Armagnac and removed any threat of pretenders/claims to the throne. He also maintained control of his most supportive nobles and the favor of the trade guilds in Bayonne

events.fullText:
## A Modest Proposal

Centule IV, seeing his people struggle for natural resources, sends a messenger to the King of Portugal. The message involves an agreement for trading rights and promises of fair pricing.

Among those requests, a secondary secret letter is slipped to the court

||Dear King, I've heard many reports of your inflaming tensions with the Castilian Crown. The County of Armagnac would be proud to support Portugese campaigns and blockade the Castilian crown from trading in our market. Let us know the outcome of your decision post-haste||

events.snippet:
# Domini de Terraferma
The sea has always been Venice's greatest strength.

While emperors and princes concern themselves with dynastic disputes and t
events.fullText:
# Domini de Terraferma
The sea has always been Venice's greatest strength.

While emperors and princes concern themselves with dynastic disputes and territorial claims, the Republic has built its prosperity upon trade and control of the routes that connect East and West. From the Adriatic to Crete, Negroponte, and the Black Sea, the *Stato da Màr* secures the commerce upon which Venice depends. Spices from Alexandria, silk from Trebizond, timber from Dalmatia, and goods from across the Mediterranean all pass through Venetian ports.

Yet while Venice looks eastward, the mainland remains divided. Across the Veneto and Lombardy, the struggles between Guelph and Ghibelline continue to weaken cities that might otherwise prosper. Ferrara has tightened its hold over Padua, local lords compete for influence, and shifting alliances have become the norm. This raises an important question for the Republic. Many of these cities share close ties with Venice through language, commerce, and history. Their stability is not separate from our own interests. For generations Venice has focused on the sea, and rightly so. However, there may come a time when the Republic must pay greater attention to the Terraferma. This is not a matter of conquest for its own sake, but of securing the routes, markets, and communities upon which Venetian prosperity depends.

The Republic cannot ignore developments around it. Genoa remains a rival at sea, new powers continue to emerge in the East, and imperial authority in Italy grows weaker with each passing decade. In such circumstances, Venice must consider not only the security of its maritime possessions, but also the future of the mainland cities closest to it.

If Venice does not take an interest in the future of the Veneto, others certainly will.

### Treaty Of Munich

Despite tensions over the Inheritance of Tyrol, the Kaiser, the Pope and the Habsburg duke has met in Munich to discuss potentia
events.fullText:
### Treaty Of Munich

Despite tensions over the Inheritance of Tyrol, the Kaiser, the Pope and the Habsburg duke has met in Munich to discuss potential peace between the two houses.

After much negotiations, these terms would be agreed upon:

- Tyrol shall remain a vassal of Austria
- The Emperor shall appoint a suitable count for Tyrol
- Austria shall cede Eastern Upper Swabia and Aargau to Bavaria
- The Pope donates 500 gold to the emperor, to smooth things over.

Peace in our time.

## THE TOWN OF MEISSEN

The capital of the Duchy of Meissen has long been a small and minor settlement, but with the recent growth in the power of the Duchy of Meissen, and the House of Wettin who reside in Meissen proper, the settlement has grown to become a proper town, one on the level of Freiberg or Leipzig within the territory of Meissen.

events.author: Tartarus
events.snippet:
## MAD BEATS ON A MAD DRUM
The beat of the drum sounds throughout the land. All five waves of the mind guide the rhythmic pattern, as the electrical i
events.fullText:
## MAD BEATS ON A MAD DRUM
The beat of the drum sounds throughout the land. All five waves of the mind guide the rhythmic pattern, as the electrical impulses in the brain shudder and splinter and the body shakes. Babies in their cots froth at the mouth, their mother's milk rancid and sour, clotting in the breast. For the men, wine is like vinegar, and there is little joy to be had, instead a dispassionate disregard for the daily activities of normal life.

Along the coast, few fishermen are to be seen, and in the desert, the Bedouins hide from the sun. All is not normal in the land, and the Sultan is nowhere to be seen, and of those who do not hear the drums, are left with only rumours of their Lord playing girlish games in silk.

Fewer and fewer travellers are permitted in these lands, and those that do often do not return.

**Fall of the Breton Duchy**
<@460814348487753730> 
There would be no great triumph as there had been when the Black Ankou smashed french forces some ten years ago. Instead french armies would march over ruined castles and undermanned garrisons, the lands of the east still stripped bare from past wars. An English relief never would come would see the fall of the capital of Brittany and a collapse of resistance. 

Duchess Janned who would be known as the last Breton Princess and her Husband the very brief Duke of Brittany Jean Luxembourg <@261290667106369536> would be executed in Paris after a very prefuctory trial. A crowd of mourners watching on including ironically Jeanne de Flandre the Flame who sees the French kings illegal seizure of her sons birthright with rage, quoted to have said 

> ***"Better an rival in Brittany than a empty Frenchman alone"***

Before departing with her supporters for flanders....

Jeanne De Clisson meanwhile departing to <@1106651545296195655> England to swear her Black Fleet to the English navy as privateers swearing to continue her ruthless campaign until the day she dies

Brittany itself would have its Ducal titles revoked and the ancient independent bishoprics squashed as havens of pirates and crime. Though the age of Breton romanticism would see a campaign of banditry and aysmertical warfare be waged by Breton peasant communities in the deep western forests which will take years of french peacekeeping to fully quell. Music and stories of such bandits and the Heroines of the first and second war going on to influence much of later Breton nationalism.

𝔊𝔬𝔡 𝔖𝔞𝔳𝔢 𝔅𝔯𝔦𝔱𝔱𝔞𝔫𝔶, 𝔊𝔬𝔡 𝔖𝔞𝔳𝔢 𝔉𝔯𝔞𝔫𝔠𝔢 𝔣𝔬𝔯 𝔱𝔥𝔢 𝔖𝔦𝔫𝔰 𝔱𝔥𝔢𝔶 𝔥𝔞𝔳𝔢 𝔠𝔬𝔪𝔪𝔦𝔱𝔱𝔢𝔡.

**(Brittany Dead)**

events.author: Rishi
events.snippet:
**The Orsini War for the North**
From 1347 to 1353, the Kingdoms of Denmark and Sweden were at war over Papal desire to seize Denmark for the Orsinis.
events.fullText:
**The Orsini War for the North**
From 1347 to 1353, the Kingdoms of Denmark and Sweden were at war over Papal desire to seize Denmark for the Orsinis. Due to many factors, this war was exceptionally brutal despite ultimately ending in stalemate.

In 1353, the war would commence and despite early naval victories by Denmark, the Swedes would eventually cross into Zealand on the backs of rented Hanseatic boats. The Swedes would use a clever tactic in which a diversionary troop would draw defenders to fight off a chevauchee on Odensee, leaving Roskilde free to march upon. Within the year, despite victories on land and sea, the war looked bleak for Denmark. 

This would become doubly the case as a plague would sweep Europe, killing thousands of embittered soldiers and the peasants that support them. Denmark would choose coin rather than soldiers to change the course of the conflict. A bribe ensured that Hanseatic fleets desert their former employers and the now larger Danish force converged upon the Swedish siege of Copenhagen. Unfortunately, luck would not be on their side as Sweden would win despite their condition. 

The increasing severity of the plague and the failures on the battlefield left the Danish creditors with little confidence in the King and shortly after, they would retract their funding. With no funds left to finish the war, the King would await his dethroning in the castle of Copenhagen. Yet, as fate would have it, the war would once again would turn. The last minute arrival of the Brandenburger army would send the Swedes back across the Oresund. But once again, the victory was not long lived. The Swedes, over the course of the next year, gathered together a force which ultimately pushed the Brandenburger army back across the strait. Unable to cross due to Danish naval supremacy, the war would end in status quo.

# IT'S TRUE.

*He had done it. Karamids, at least for a time, was secure.*

*He had borne an heir, he had found h
events.fullText:
# THIS HOLY LAND IS MADE FOR YOU...

# IT'S TRUE.

*He had done it. Karamids, at least for a time, was secure.*

*He had borne an heir, he had found his great victory, not against the Eretnids, but rather the Candarids.*

*His advisors diplomacy with the many factions of Anatolia have granted the Karamanids enough maneuverability so as they would not be destroyed, neutral enough that no one faction can move against them without inviting the ire of the others. Not only that but many of them gave their own coin for Karamanid aid, being the engine that had propelled his tribe to power. Soon enough, he is to surpass the Eternids as the supreme Beylik within the region. Once that comes to pass, he holds no delusion that they too shall be fought.*

 *He has conquered many of the other smaller beyliks under heel, and soon enough the Ottomans and Hamadids would be vassalized under heel, even now he makes offers of money and land to them, and even should they refuse it shall be done by force. But for now he bides his time, and Karamanids would be made permanent unto history.*

events.snippet: On the palace in Sarai, Grand Prince Ivan II "the Breaker of Chains" has done it. After facing Khan Borkhuu Tomsk for close to a decade, campaign acro
events.fullText:
On the palace in Sarai, Grand Prince Ivan II "the Breaker of Chains" has done it. After facing Khan Borkhuu Tomsk for close to a decade, campaign across the steppes, facing rival tribal chiefs seeing opportunity trying to defeat the Muscovite army, the Khan finally admited defeat.

Ivan II went to places he never would have thought of, to the shores of the Black Sea, to the foot of the Caucasus, to the deserts of Central Asia. The Khan was one thing, if not knowing when to give up.

However, this time, the mongol would kneel. The Ryazan lands would be given to the Grand Principality, and the Tartar Yoke formally abolished. No more would Russians be enslaved!

Grand Prince Ivan II is hailed back in Muscovy as a hero, known as Razrushitel' tsepey, "Breaker of Chains." Hail be to Ivan II!

Following the death of his brother in a hunting accident, Erik Estridsen found himself suddenly in the spotlight as the new heir t
events.fullText:
**The Wayward Son**
Following the death of his brother in a hunting accident, Erik Estridsen found himself suddenly in the spotlight as the new heir to Denmark. For the last 18 years of his life, he was nothing more than a second son making a career far away from his family. And he has done well, rising to even become the general of the Holy Roman Armies.

Despite his experience with responsibility, he seems unwilling to accept his new role as heir. Even today, a whole year after his brother's death, he has not returned to his rightful place in Denmark. The King worries for the dynasty's future...

events.author: Tartarus
events.snippet:
## EYES OF GEMS
Twinkling in the moonlit night, wandering poet's subject's crazed eyes catch the glint of gems. Drifting in and out of fitful sleep, t
events.fullText:
## EYES OF GEMS
Twinkling in the moonlit night, wandering poet's subject's crazed eyes catch the glint of gems. Drifting in and out of fitful sleep, they are caught in vice grip by diamonds, opals, sapphires, rubies and gold: endless gold. Locked deep beneath antiquated pyramids, the rumours of hordes and hordes of treasure bring forth endless hauntologies to the living. They set out into the desert and the sea, hoping, wishing to find but a hint of some priceless mineral in the endless soft sands, and are left wanting more and more: whether they find what they seek or not.

In Cairo, where men's madness is sharper, and their acuity is refined into bloodlust and greed and political ambition, their eyes shimmer just the same, though in the sun this turns into a bloodiness, a hunger, cruel and unforgiving.

Who can bear the sickness that seeps into the soil of this land?

events.fullText:
### Conquest of Utrecht
Long have the Bishops of Utrecht mettled in the affairs of the Hollandish realm, but following the success of the Berger <@792904515795550218> war against the County of Cleaves, the seat of the Bishop in the town of Utrecht has been ceded to Holland alongside the village of Amersfoort. The bishop has been reseated Zwolle across Zuiderzee, vowing to return to his historic seat while the burghers of Holland prepare to take the conflict back across the Zuiderzee alongside their Frisian vassals.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
The Western City

Nikolsk had fallen into the hands of Great Perm some decades ago after the defeat of the Novgorodians of Vyatka, but since then it h
events.fullText:
The Western City

Nikolsk had fallen into the hands of Great Perm some decades ago after the defeat of the Novgorodians of Vyatka, but since then it had languished. Most of them had stayed there, holed up in their hovels and disheveled fortifications, but all the stolen wealth of the town had been returned to the local peoples and the Ugric peoples -much disfavouring these former pirates- refused to visit the city, leading to a sharp economic decline.
By 1364 Great Perm had come to a decision; the poor inhabitants of Nikolsk would -with their land- be sold to Muscovy, in return for a sizeable sum of course. For the Komi who would rather have been rid of it in the first place, it was quite the deal. For the Muscovites a town on the eastern frontier for such a petty price as the tribes demanded was likewise a no-brainer.
*And so it was sold...*

events.author: Tartarus

## BEARING CLOAKS CRIMSON RED AND SCARLET
The men, riding on horses, arrived. They were bearing cloaks crimson red and scarlet, and the banner of Syri
events.fullText:
## BEARING CLOAKS CRIMSON RED AND SCARLET
The men, riding on horses, arrived. They were bearing cloaks crimson red and scarlet, and the banner of Syrians, Turkomen, Egyptians and Kurds, and the banner of Islam. They did not seek an audience, simply informed those in this land of a new order: their presence, to be made permanent.

When approached, all that could be heard was the sound of trumpets and drums, and they would assault any who sought to speak with them, and left behind shit and bones where they travelled.

All that was clear was that more of them were to come.

(WAR DEC ON CILICIA/CYPRUS FOR CILICIA. 1 YEAR WARNING - JULY 1367)

<@698400527834415145> <@302128861209165824> <@150411715757080576> <@1216097200401154181>

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
**Succession of the Grand Prince**

For near thirty years Yövgin' had ruled as Prince of Great Perm, but age comes for us all. In the spring of 1363 Y
events.fullText:
**Succession of the Grand Prince**

For near thirty years Yövgin' had ruled as Prince of Great Perm, but age comes for us all. In the spring of 1363 Yövgin' Rjuriklön would die in his old age. While he had doubtless ruled capably, his cruelty and temper was well known... it was men like him that made sure the chieftains never gave one of them too much power - with absolute power comes absolute greed, as the saying goes.
While his strong son had long been presumed to follow him as Prince of Perm, Öl'öksan had died just a few years earlier - mauled by a bear during a hunting trip.

The proceedings to elect a new prince were rather usual, though the outcome was somewhat unique; Rayö of Nytva, a Komi-Permyan chieftain was chosen to rule as Grand Prince of Perm. While out of the ordinary, she was undoubtedly capable, and her years as a respected leader of her tribespeople would hopefully now reflect upon all the Komi.

events.author: darthpenance
events.snippet: Mikhail Rurikovich, 2nd youngest brother of Grand Prince Ivan II, is sent to the tribesmen of Perm in Cherdyn. The Grand Prince has noticed that the t
events.fullText:
Mikhail Rurikovich, 2nd youngest brother of Grand Prince Ivan II, is sent to the tribesmen of Perm in Cherdyn. The Grand Prince has noticed that the tribesmen have been more active as of lately, and he comes with an offer.

Muscovy shall assist the tribesmen of Perm in building a city in Cherdyn as well as developing it to be a center of civilization in the east, in exchange for becoming a protectorate of Muscovy, against the steppes and Siberia, as a righteous man, he seeks to enlighten then. He also invites Countess Rayo Ol'oksanlon to visit Moskva and see its beauties.

events.author: Cod In A Pod
events.snippet:
**Foundation of Lviv University**

After decades - no, a century, of being torn away from the rest of the Orthodox World, the Rada has authorized an e
events.fullText:
**Foundation of Lviv University**

After decades - no, a century, of being torn away from the rest of the Orthodox World, the Rada has authorized an envoy to Constantinople. There, the Halych-Volhynian delegates were warmly received at the Imperial Court - despite difficult history and difficulty understanding each other at first, the sense of brotherhood prevailed and brought both sides close almost immediately.

The envoy, tasked with establishing relations at first, has gone a step further and has asked for reciprocal delegation to the expanded city of Lviv - the capital of the Kingdom. The response has left the delegation speechless - not only has the Emperor himself promised to assist in assembling a delegation of nobles and theologians, but he has diverted his funds to help establish a University to promote teachings of the word of God in the Kingdom. 

After arriving a month later in Lviv, the procession has consecrated the site and brought an icon of Saints Cyril and Methodius, brothers who have gifted the written Slavonic language, itterations of which are spoken throughout Ruthenia, to it. With that, the construction of the Univesity has officially begun.

The dirst University in the region, one of the very few in the Orthodox World - it has seemingly become the true crowning achievement of the rule of King Trojden. The difficult times of plagues, wars and uncertain future are definitely past the people and the world will soon enough make sense once more - not the same as it was a century ago, but in a way, perhaps, a bit more reassuring of the things to come tomorrow.

<@302128861209165824>

events.fullText:

Rayö has died, and after much though her daughter **Opoch** has been elected ruler of Grand Perm. While her predecessor's rule was not a long one, Rayö's role in diplomacy with Muscovy is certain to have a lasting legacy.

events.author: Hosumaija
events.snippet:
The Yasak of Perm

In 1370 an agreement would be reached between Prince Ivan II of Muscovy and the Council of Clan Elders of Great Perm, headed by new
events.fullText:
The Yasak of Perm

In 1370 an agreement would be reached between Prince Ivan II of Muscovy and the Council of Clan Elders of Great Perm, headed by newly crowned Opoch.

After a long period of true independence, the Komi people have once more sworn to provide yasak to the Rus'... though now to Muscovy instead of Novgorod. In return for yasak, a tribute of their valuable Siberian furs, Muscovy promises vast investments in the remote region. Especially of note during the trip to Muscovy as a topic of conversation are the churches. Grand, beautiful, unrivalled by any buildings in Perm they make an incredible impression upon the Komi delegates, but it remains to be seen if Christianity will have any success in penetrating Perm.

## 500 TURKISH CHILDREN ARRIVE IN SICILY
Sent by the Sultan to his wards in Tunis to the recently conquered island of Sicily, 500 young Turkoman children all under the age of 16 and over the age of 8 are sent. They carry small, ceremonial copper daggers, and are trained as whirling dervishes. Curiously, they all bear the name Otman.

events.author: GMB
events.fullText:
# THE RED DEVIL IS DEAD! THANK GOD! IT IS A SIGN SENT BY GOD! # 

<@763652399784919080> <@190640828954181632> <@215639063502258176> <@261290667106369536> 
<@306458253674545153> 

Ruprecht "the Red" was dead. Sent likely to hell for his treachery. But he did redeem himself in many ways. His most honorable deed was certainly saving his Grace Karel IV from certain death after the battle of Mosbach. Although imprisoned by his brother after news of a further plot of escaping his House arrest had been revelead by the Habsburgs, Ruprecht did not give up. 

He managed himself to dress up as a Monk, joined a Holy Order and escaped to Mainz. There he would rise thorugh the ranks with the aid of the other Ruprecht, "the Robbed" and became the clear successor for the Prince-Bishopric of Mainz.

Impressive indeed, he never gave up and kept on fighting. Once in power he went on a spree of conquest, angering many in the region. And during a battle, he would persih. Much like his namesake he would fall in Red. 

But Rudolf contemplated if things could have been different. Once uppon a time they had fought together against Ludwig IV in a bid to ensure their inheritance right with success through the treaty of Pavia. 

They split their lands, were happy, but greed overtook the brother and this would be his eventual downfall. Yet one thing is for certain: **RUPRECHT THE RED NEVER GAVE UP**

And it was with this that Rudolf thought to himself: 

*"He might have betrayed me... Aye... But he was still my brother and he was a Wittelsbach through and through. Honor kept him alive, greed killed. I hated him yes. But I loved him aswell. Rest in Peace, in whichever hell you were sent to little brother."*

`"With tears in his eyes, Rudolf took to his Messanger... Get me a Quill. I need to write to the new Archbishop. My brothers remains ought to return to Heidelberg."`

events.fullText:
# CRISIS STRIKES AGAIN! A GRANDSON DIES! # 

The loss of Ruprecht "the Red" was perhaps a small victory, but perhaps god punishes vengence and anger in twisted ways. With the death of Ruprecht came also news of Rudolfs Grandson: Maximilian Josef had suddenly died... 

The line was still secure with a new Heir: Adolph von Wittelsbach, but at the end it was still a heavy blow to the House of Wittelsbach... Could a succession crisis emerge from this? Who knows... 

<@763652399784919080>

events.author: Murlock
events.fullText:
**The Pope is Dead. <:Keys_of_heaven:1082042509359845458> **
@here

Pope Ioannes XXIII has died. His support helped destroy the Orsini, and his final efforts were spent attempting to unite a crusade against the **Mamluks** and **Tunis**, who have attacked Christians in the East. He will be remembered fondly across **Christendom**.

But his death has sparked a **Schism**, where no true Pope can be agreed upon.

However, in a bastardly move, the **French Court** demands the return of the Papacy to **France**, after it had been moved back to **Rome** under the Orsini.

The **Italian Cardinals** stand against such actions. The Papacy shall and should remain in **Rome**, where it may exist beyond the intervention of kings and emperors.

This is false and against the efforts of the former pope who fought against french and Orsini influence. This is deeply dissapointing.

The new pope agrees, more against the Cardinals in France

While the Pope commands armies against the Mamaluks, the French and there supporters are scheming and not supporting the Crusade!

The Pope is outraged at the actions of the French Faction, and as such recognizes Edward IV as the true King of France, and asks the Emperor <@763652399784919080> to find <@460814348487753730> and bring him to justice for his crimes.

events.author: GMB
events.snippet:
# RUDOLF II "THE RESTORED" HAS DIED AFTER ENSURING THE TRIER-MAINZ WAR ENDED WITH A WHITE PEACE # 

<@190640828954181632> <@261290667106369536> <@7636
events.fullText:
# RUDOLF II "THE RESTORED" HAS DIED AFTER ENSURING THE TRIER-MAINZ WAR ENDED WITH A WHITE PEACE # 

<@190640828954181632> <@261290667106369536> <@763652399784919080> <@137101075411894272> <@306458253674545153> 

Rudolf II "the Restored" will certainly go down in history for his deeds. Deposed by his own brother yet restored by his Uncle. A fighter for Kaiser Stephen von Wittelsbach, a skilled diplomat and achiever of reconnection. And even in the end he redeemed himself... Through nothing else than repenting for his brother. 

But even with the success of brokering the peace between Trier and Mainz, things were set for problems. For the news of his brother and his grandson dying... Brought immense pain to the Just ruler. 

Calm as always he died peacefully in his bed, returning just after his diplomatic deeds. 

Many may call him an average diplomat, having failed to ensure peace with France, yet historians have a soft spot for the Just Count Palatinate, that restored himself into the pages of History Books. 

His daughter Anna "the Prize" now rules, with her Wittelsbach Husband Albrecht, Duke of Bavaria-Chiemgau. Although her authority stems more from her Son Adolph, she has been shown to be a promising figure. For many a genius of her Time, a top administrator and diplomat, some even whispper that she was the true power behind Rudolf's reign

events.author: GMB
events.fullText:
# THE FUNERAL OF THE WITTELSBACH BROTHERS # 

<@763652399784919080> <@190640828954181632> <@215639063502258176> <@385875387608989714> <@112960565344804864> <@306458253674545153> <@137101075411894272> <@288410456526422016> <@420614608399761419> <@792904515795550218> <@150411715757080576> 

Countess Palatine Anna "the Prize" von Wittelsbach's reign has seen better days. Her first act as ruler was to further decentralize the eastern provinces, but it was also a way to ensure that the nobles loyal to her Uncle Ruprecht "the Red" received some concessions and accept the new Countess to be able to rule effectivelly. A masterstroke of genius for sure, something that truly shows her capabilities. 

Yet with this also came her second act as Countess. For with it also came the creation of large ceremony at Heidelberg. There the men and woman of her new Realm would all pay their respects to the combined funeral of the two Wittelsbach Brothers. The event is a large ceremony, members from across the whole empire, from wittelsbachs to Luxemburgs were invited. Even the Habsburgs were invited to come, for it was time to show that this was a new age for the Palatinate. One of unity. Even the pope was invited... But there was an exception: The Savoians were explicitly not invited, for the duke had insulted the new Countess with a Bastard child. With this she united both factions of the Court under her. Those loyal to Ruprecht and those loyal to her Father Rudolf. With a speech she held, the new Countess Elector would unite her Nobles and Clergy alike:

**"The two Wittelsbach Brothers have fallen. Now comes the time for healing. Restoration is now here and Red blood for those who seek to destroy us. May god guide us in these harsh times, for the Papacy may be divided, but these Two Rival Brothers are now reunited... In heaven."**

# The Treviso Question
Relations between Venice and the Prince-Bishopric of Aquileia have become increasingly strained in recent years. While the Repu
events.fullText:
# The Treviso Question
Relations between Venice and the Prince-Bishopric of Aquileia have become increasingly strained in recent years. While the Republic has long preferred diplomacy and commerce to territorial disputes, developments in Treviso and the surrounding territories have drawn growing concern within Venetian circles. Treviso occupies an important position between the lagoon and the interior of northern Italy. Its roads, markets, and rivers connect the mainland to the Adriatic, making its stability a matter of interest not only to its inhabitants, but to every state engaged in regional trade.

Many within the Republic now question whether Aquileian rule is capable of providing that stability. The Prince-Bishops continue to assert authority over lands increasingly distant from their direct control. What may once have been accepted as legitimate ecclesiastical governance is now viewed by many Venetian merchants as an obstacle to effective administration. The Republic has made no formal claim upon Treviso. Nevertheless, it is becoming increasingly clear that Venice can no longer regard events there as matters of no concern. The security of the mainland and the security of the Republic are closely connected.

## In old tradition - Shared Lands, A United House in Savoy

*The House de Savoie has in it's long history, often acquired titles and quite regularely
events.fullText:
## In old tradition - Shared Lands, A United House in Savoy

*The House de Savoie has in it's long history, often acquired titles and quite regularely these were not directly held by the Head of the Dynasty, no instead they were entrusted to brothers and cousins, establishing Cadet Lines, holding their own fiefs, though sworn to the Count and now Duke in Savoy.*

*The same shall now happen to the **Duchy of Aosta**, while once of high importance to the Counts of Savoy, due to it's ducal status, with the Savoyard lands themselves having been elevated to ducal honours, there is no more need to directly administrate these lands, which can only be reached through the passes. As such, Duke Amedé the Green has decreed that the lands of Aosta shall pass to his younger brother **Louis de Savoie**, who shall establish a **cadet line** of the House de Savoie.*

*The middle brother, **John de Savoie** is passed over as with Amedé only having daughters and Savoy subscribing to **Salic Law**, as per the will of the nobility when they elected Èmon the Pacific over his niece only four decades ago. The House shall stand united, as ever, in these tumulteous times.*

events.snippet:
**The Twin headed Eagle over Brittany  **
<@460814348487753730> <@1106651545296195655> <@763652399784919080> 

If ever there are two things the nobili
events.fullText:
**The Twin headed Eagle over Brittany  **
<@460814348487753730> <@1106651545296195655> <@763652399784919080> 

If ever there are two things the nobility in every realm from the distant middle kingdom whose emperors are said to be immortal to the banks of Portugal would chatter upon it is the talk of war and of marriage of which ironically both have a tendency of causing. In Nantes there is such talk of both and an answer to both as well, Holy roman princes of Luxembourg and Wittelsbach both offering marriage offers to the young Duchess which would in effect create a new dynasty and end the Dreux line. Despite resistance from Blois who could have their children marry her and so keep the line alive the nobles of the court outweigh them, knowing the desperate need for military allies in the face of French aggression is far more important than maintaining bloodlines right now. 

Though there would be much debate as the lords of Rohan argued in favour of Luxembourg for the maintenance of laws and connection to trade while the remaining scions of the Clisson family battered would look to a more powerful ally (though questionable how much said ally would in truth help) in the form of the Holy Roman Emperors themselves. However the Clisson’s estates have been mostly destroyed or sold off to fuel blood crazed vengeance campaigns and so in the end the Luxembourg offer would be the one taken to the regent Jeanne who would under said pressure reluctantly agree, although would inform their allies/liege Edward the III in London while she remains in parliament there to keep the alliance alive.

So it is that the weathered realm summons the effort to begin to arrange for a grand wedding at the newly rebuilt Cathédrale Saint-Pierre-et-Saint-Paul at Nantes even as the bride herself Janed Dreux reaction is more akin to a caged lion being forced to be the pet of a child. Only the herculean effort of an army of servants and an army of well just an army to keep her within her quatres and not run off into the woods as she so oft is threatening to do, most trying to politely keep the groom away from the bride for now on the quite reasonable assumption she may try to kill him. A German dignitary quoted to have said  

> *“are all the women of this land filled with naught but bile and spite?!” *

**Though ofcourse with such grand preparations undergoing the king of France would easily be able to take note, the possible threat of Bohemian intervention now increased, and actions that could be taken to stop it. So to in England and if others opposing such a match would wish to intercede, they may well try.... **

events.snippet:
**Tribal Politics!**

The decision of the Grand Elder Council of Perm to pay yasak to Russia was not a unanimous one, and the opponents of it were *ve
events.fullText:
**Tribal Politics!**

The decision of the Grand Elder Council of Perm to pay yasak to Russia was not a unanimous one, and the opponents of it were *very* opposed indeed. A group of conspirators soon gathered and began plotting to reverse the treaty and rebel against Muscovite overlordship. The planned church in Perm is a point of particular grievance, and even among the general population scepticism and hostility alike is plenty abound.

After nearly two years of infighting in the council, several duels to the death and chieftains fleeing from justice with their tribes, the conspirators were put down, but the blood spilt will surely not be forgotten soon among their kith and kin.

To the esteemed Elector Friedrich III of Meissen,

I write to you with an offer of marriage between my youn
events.fullText:
**Bethrotal Between Denmark and Meissen**

To the esteemed Elector Friedrich III of Meissen,

I write to you with an offer of marriage between my youngest daughter and your chivalrous heir. Young Helen is well educated in matters of administration and ladyhood being raised in the Holy Roman Court at Munich. She is well acquainted with the politics and life of the Holy Roman Empire, living there for 17 years alongside her brother, the Commander of the Kaiser Guard. If this arrangement is suitable, we shall both travel to Munich to bring her from the court of the Holy Roman Emperor.

Regards,
King Valdemar of Denmark

** The Battle of Alba De Tormes **

<@298192682554228738> The Portugese King foolishly attacked Castile and attempted to enforce his claim on Galicia.
events.fullText:
** The Battle of Alba De Tormes **

<@298192682554228738> The Portugese King foolishly attacked Castile and attempted to enforce his claim on Galicia. The result was predictable. The Portugese army was put to rout at Alba de Tormes, and Castilian armies marched all the way to the gates of Lisbon. A lesson was taught: Minor monetary reparations, a concession of defeat, and the dismantling of a border fort in Elvas were demanded. Nothing serious. However, the Portugese nobility's reaction to their King's failure was so severe, and the King's forces so depleted, that he was deposed and usurped! An admittedly hilarious fall from grace considering everything that has happened in the last 20 years. That being said, this could prove an interesting situation, we may smell opportunity here. However, there are some domestic issues to resolve first...

events.snippet:
**# The War Pope <:war:1139530115806658661> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XOLd1z11DpQ
@here

To the **North**, the Italians pledge allegiance
events.fullText:
**# The War Pope <:war:1139530115806658661> **
-# https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XOLd1z11DpQ
@here

To the **North**, the Italians pledge allegiance to the **French Court** against the **Roman Cardinals**. <:machiavelli:1336548734825140293> 

To the **East**, the **Byzantine Empire**, in league with Muslims, destroys the rightful crusade, while the **Mamluks** defeat the crusaders in the Holy Land, pushing them back from reclaiming **Jerusalem**. <:Byz:837204651618074645> 

To the **South**, **Sicily** remains under the grip of the **Tunis Caliphate**, defeating the crusaders in their attempts to reclaim the island. 🕌 

To the **West**, the **French Court** denounces the rightful **Roman Church** and wages a war of carnage upon the Empire, killing hundreds of thousands in an act of barbarism. <:france:1209832243896524830> 

**Catholicism is besieged on all fronts.** The Roman Church faces a crisis never before seen in the history of **Christendom**, and yet many in this division still support the enemies of the Church. Divided, confused, and consumed by greed, they care not for the holiness of the faith nor the unity of the Church, but instead side with the **French Court** and its puppets, while it seeks the blood of thousands across the Empire.

When the Cardinals gathered to select a Pope, there was no other choice. It was **Phelix**, the man who had called for war and blood when this all began. And so the vote was cast in a single sitting, despite the absence of the **French** and **Neapolitan Cardinals**.

** <:war:1139530115806658661>  The War Pope had been chosen <:war:1139530115806658661> **

> "My brothers and sisters in Christ, look around you and bear witness to the age we now endure. Christendom burns. The enemies of our faith gather at every horizon, and the Church of Rome, the very rock upon which Saint Peter stood, is besieged as never before. To the North, princes betray the unity of Christ and kneel to foreign ambition. To the East, the enemies of the Cross strike down crusaders and mock the holy mission to restore Jerusalem. To the South, Sicily remains in chains beneath heathen rule. And to the West, Christian fights Christian while kingdoms drown themselves in blood for pride and earthly power.
> 
> And what do many do in this hour of trial? They hesitate. They divide. They barter holiness for gold and unity for ambition.
> 
> **No more.**
> 
> You all stand before a choice: to take up the sword and defend the Church from its enemies, or to falter and allow Satan to consume our world.
> 
> Do not be tempted by greed and the material substances of this Earth, for they are fleeting and hollow. For behind the curtain, God and Mary, the Mother of God <:mary3:1126382749612249108> , await your judgment day.
> 
> When that hour comes, no king, no gold, nor earthly title shall save your soul. Only your faith, your sacrifice, and your devotion to Christ and His Church shall stand before Heaven. Choose now whether you shall stand as defenders of Christendom, or kneel before the darkness that seeks to consume it.
> 
> For when wolves surround the flock, the shepherd must not tremble. He must act. For if Christendom is besieged, then Christendom shall answer. **And by God, we shall endure.**"

"The Northern Italian princes are in league with the French Anti-Pope and are working to undermine the Church. The Church has stood by Naples and has provided **10 gold a month** to support your crusade, while these heathens support the political games of the French King, who has only worsened the crusade and sponsored the defeat of the reclamation of **Sicily**.

Do not create further division. We must rein in the Northern Italians and seek to further unify the Church. Do not allow it to shatter and weaken."

events.author: Bas1leus
events.snippet:
** The Sale of Gibraltar **

In the wake of the recent conflict over the island of Sicily between the Iberian Kingdoms, Naples, Tunis, and Morocco, th
events.fullText:
** The Sale of Gibraltar **

In the wake of the recent conflict over the island of Sicily between the Iberian Kingdoms, Naples, Tunis, and Morocco, the tentative stalemate that was established upon the ascension of Alfonso XI between Castile and Morocco was finally broken. However, with the Sultan Abu Hammu Marinid succeeding the throne and becoming a full fledged ruler in his own right, the Christian monarch of Castile has proposed a method of finally putting an end to the centuries-long conflict of the Reconquista, not by force, but through diplomacy. The two great states have agreed that Morocco will lease the province of Gibraltar to the Crown of Castile for an indefinite term, and the province of Malaga will be fully integrated into the Emirate of Granada. In return, the Crown of Castile will pay a direct sum of 500 golden ducats to the Sultan's coffers. May this amicable resolution finally put an end to this bloody chapter of history, and let us tend to a peaceful future.

events.snippet:
# Calls for reform

Prince-Bishop Andreas Reuchlin sends a letter to the Emperor Jan z Lucembursko in order to set a motion for strengthening the HRE
events.fullText:
# Calls for reform

Prince-Bishop Andreas Reuchlin sends a letter to the Emperor Jan z Lucembursko in order to set a motion for strengthening the HRE against outside threats,

||“To the Holy Roman Emperor Jan z Lucembursko,

Since our failures in Lorraine and the Low Countries, it has become extremely apparent that we lack the strength we need to push down those who would usurp lands rightfully within our reach. While many of us on the frontiers of the empire will give the service of our lands and people, there are many that would enjoy the luxuries of the protection we offer without such sacrifice.

Without a military backed by the Empire as a whole and no cost to those who do not commit their realms to the wider good of the empire we stand no chance against the kingdoms that will be our downfall if we do not act now.

As the Prince-Bishopric of Köln, we shall pledge our resolve to keep our dioceses within the rightful realm it should be but without support we are limited in how far we can prevent the Low Countries falling to foreign meddling. I wish to ask for the electors and influential members of our empire to meet in order to set a precedent for our future.

I will also be setting in line the traitors of our holiness in Italy as my duties as Arch-Chancellor of Italy will require a more direct hand.

Yours Sincerely,
Prince-Bishop Andreas Reuchlin, Arch-Chancellor of Italy”||

events.author: Cayde
events.snippet:
# The Loss of Kyburg
After the great defeat at Mossbach, the Habsburg forces were left depleted and the treasury itself had dwindled to barely above m
events.fullText:
# The Loss of Kyburg
After the great defeat at Mossbach, the Habsburg forces were left depleted and the treasury itself had dwindled to barely above maintenance. Albert, seeking to stabilize his realm, reached out to the Anjou's of Hungary ||<@423689694258397195> ||, for a defensive pact. In exchange for cessation of toll fees in Wien, and an agreement of support against the rebellious nobles of Transylvania, the Hungarian King agreed to Albert's proposal. While this defensive pact never saw itself called upon, it served as a stabilizer of Habsburg borders, especially in Austria & Styria, their core lands. Yet, this eastern alliance would not serve to defend the Habsburg ancestral lands in Aargau, for an invasion by the cantons, led by burghers of Zurich. With no levies available to secure Kyburg, the burghers of Zurich seized the opportunity to invade and capture the town. The town was sacked, with its retainer walls torn down, all without a single iota of resistance from Wien. Albert took this as a blow to his ego and prestige, but in the grand scheme viewed it as nothing more than a small loss, while his ambitions lie on the crown of the entire Empire itself.

-# *Pictured above is Schloss Kyburg, which still lies in Habsburg hands despite the fall of the town itself and most of its surrounding territories*

events.author: Marcus
events.snippet:
Reflection on war

The young King Erik has had a difficult upbringing, in his youth he remembers being surrounded by his mother and various nobles and
events.fullText:
Reflection on war

The young King Erik has had a difficult upbringing, in his youth he remembers being surrounded by his mother and various nobles and officials, he was to be King after all, but he was not yet, his mother was the one who manipulated the court, controlled the regent council. Why she saw fit to plan and scheme on the behalf of the Orsini even after her husbands death is unknown, but her schemes did not benefit the realm or anybody at all really. Consumed by greed, she launched a campaign against Denmark, shattering the trust and relations between our realms, thousands of lives lost...

This has for long weighted on the conscience of King Erik, frequent nightmares disturb his sleep, voices tell him he had responsibility, it has recently even started to impact his everyday life. An incident of great embarrassment was the breaking point for him. In front of a foreign delegation he fell into what seemed a bout of insanity and shot an arrow into the leg of King Pedro of Portugal, who was staying at the Swedish court.

All this has led the King to seek a meeting with King Valdemar of Denmark, he sincerely wishes to restore ties between Denmark and Sweden, ties that will guarantee peace and prosperity for our realms.
