**A New King**
At the age of 62 King Valdemar was succeeded by his son, King Erik VII. Erik, much like his father, comes from a military background, long serving as general of the Holy Roman Armies. Unlike his father, he lacks a penchant for administration. His warlike nature and zeal for battle will no doubt lead him to seek conquests abroad. May he lead Denmark to Glory!

As Friedrich had once discussed with the Emperor himself in a correspondence years ago, the foreseen and feared threat has arisen. Ro
events.fullText:
# Robber Barons

As Friedrich had once discussed with the Emperor himself in a correspondence years ago, the foreseen and feared threat has arisen. Robber Barons have entrenched themselves in the mountains of the Alps, levying heavy taxes on the valuable silver flowing through these routes, and growing fat with the riches of their plunder. Friedrich, for the first time in nearly a decade, is preparing himself to ride out for war, to crush these Robber barons and remind all those who question the authority of the Archduke, most especially the nobility who paint themselves as anything more than their birthrights.

events.snippet:
# The regrowth of Koln
After the failures in Flanders and occupied nature of the coffers aimed towards war, the city of Koln face an era of stagnancy.
events.fullText:
# The regrowth of Koln
After the failures in Flanders and occupied nature of the coffers aimed towards war, the city of Koln face an era of stagnancy.

As the municipal council has taken over the treasuries usage, the city has experienced far greater growth of it's industry while clerical investments have started to dwindle, for those looking to trade a plethora of trade goods are on the market whilst the state of minor churches has started to disappoint pilgrims visiting places outside the cathedral. This resurgance has benefited the city greatly for it's people and 

More so, laws have shifted in the favour of the municipal council rather than the Domkapitel, the reduction clerical administration is noticable both within the city and outside the city. Tradition falls in the shadow of innovation, the dominance of the Clergy is starting to fade.

Such shifts in power with the absence of the Prince-Bishop could leave the state of Cologne ever so closer moving further away from the theocratic nature of law.

<@306458253674545153> <@137101075411894272> <@150411715757080576> <@792904515795550218> <@420614608399761419>

events.author: jeudlvie
events.snippet:
## Peace of Abbeville 

Young King William III had scarcely made the journey from his holdings in Wales to the palace in London, being quickly sworn i
events.fullText:
## Peace of Abbeville 

Young King William III had scarcely made the journey from his holdings in Wales to the palace in London, being quickly sworn in by the Archbishop of Canterbury following news of Edward's death in Brittany, when he was told the English Army had barely made it home. 

The country wept. The nobility rejoiced, in a bittersweet manner.

Edward was dead. 

In parliament, chaos ensued. Nobles shouted, shoved, and pushed eachother over what this could mean.

At the advice of his mother, Edward III signed the Peace of Abbeville with the King of France, Phillipe VII, conceding the last holdings on the continent and forever renouncing the Plantagenet claim to the throne of France. 

Peace at last, the Lords say. 
Peace at last. 

<@460814348487753730> <@1417260821674852363> <@1123821809704374313>

events.author: Redcola
events.fullText:
**The Great Vellum Brandenburger Guilds**
<@763652399784919080><@222439858927435776><@385875387608989714><@288410456526422016> 

Brandenburg is in terms of mercantilism an akward one, rural but not one of vast farmlands and having the need to still support the growing urban populations and infrastructure in the lands around, while having no grand cities of commerce nor industry. Leaving the land poor and politically only relevant in terms of its electorship.  

In turn seeing a certain level of creativty on display from the Hanseatic and local born merchants, creating a market of various ancillary and supporting goods that are otherwise too monopolised elswhere or too expensive to bother with. With the political crisis of the dynastic feud of the Luxembourgs and Wittlesbachs deepening along with the growing obsessiveness of the Margrave Ludwig he has commissioned and worked with his local Landtag to create a noble back guild, not one centered on a single city but as part of the wider territory on the creation of paper of all things. Outwardly grasping upon a resource that can be readily produced in the region but inwardly also attempting to give himself and the wider Wittlesbach Imperial faction control and ability to produce works, records and other documents far more easily, logistically a boon to the wider political feud... though such a tool could as well be used by others as well.

**The Imperial Law Must Stand!**

In a fiery speech (in VC) Ludwig of Brandenburg calls upon the entire weight of the imperial legal system and political apparatus to resist such a blatant attempt to qaush the electors rights who so clearly oppose the emperors interests, attempting to push away from this being a dynastic issue to instead a political one, drawing parreles to the recent civil war in France and the evil Dragon King Phillipe.

> *"THE LAW MUST STAND NONE MAY SAY A MANS NAME OR TITLE IS WITHOUT MEANING BUT OF THEIR BAD HUMOURS ALONE! IF THIS DIET DOES DECLARE ME WITHOUT BRANDENBURG I SHALL ACCEPT AND NOT A MOMENT BEFORE!"*

Joining with both his relatives and other noble lords Ludwig propositions if the emperor will not stand to negotiations as has been offered then a leauge of noble imperial interests must be met before a second France takes place
